title: Single-cell reference mapping with PCA method — RunPCAMap
code: RunPCAMap(   srt_query,   srt_ref,   query_assay = NULL,   ref_assay = srt_ref[[ref_pca]]@assay.used,   ref_pca = NULL,   ref_dims = 1:30,   ref_umap = NULL,   ref_group = NULL,   projection_method = c("model", "knn"),   nn_method = NULL,   k = 30,   distance_metric = "cosine",   vote_fun = "mean",   verbose = TRUE )

title: Single-cell reference mapping with PCA method — RunPCAMap
text: srt_query object class Seurat annotated cell types. srt_ref Seurat object count matrix representing reference object. provided, similarities calculated cells query reference objects. provided, similarities calculated within query object. query_assay assay use query object. provided, default assay query object used. ref_assay assay use reference object. provided, default assay reference object used. ref_pca character string specifying name PCA reduction reference object use calculating distance metric. NULL (default), automatically detected first PCA reduction. ref_dims numeric vector specifying dimension indices reference reduction used calculating distance metric. ref_umap character string specifying name UMAP reduction reference object. provided, first UMAP reduction found reference object used. ref_group grouping variable reference object. variable used group cells heatmap columns. provided, cells treated one group. projection_method character string specifying projection method use. Options "model" "knn". "model" selected, function try use pre-trained UMAP model reference object projection. "knn" selected, function directly find nearest neighbors using distance metric. nn_method character string specifying nearest neighbor search method use. Options "raw", "annoy", "rann". "raw" selected, function use brute-force method find nearest neighbors. "annoy" selected, function use Annoy library approximate nearest neighbor search. "rann" selected, function use RANN library approximate nearest neighbor search. provided, function choose search method based size query reference datasets. k number nearest neighbors find cell query object. distance_metric distance metric use calculating pairwise distances cells. Options include: "pearson", "spearman", "cosine", "correlation", "jaccard", "ejaccard", "dice", "edice", "hamman", "simple matching", "faith". Additional distance metrics can also used, "euclidean", "manhattan", "hamming", etc. vote_fun character string specifying function used aggregating nearest neighbors reference object. Options "mean", "median", "sum", "min", "max", "sd", "var", etc. provided, default "mean". verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE.

title: Single-cell reference mapping with PCA method — RunPCAMap
code: data(panc8_sub) panc8_sub <- standard_scop(panc8_sub) srt_ref <- panc8_sub[, panc8_sub$tech != "fluidigmc1"] srt_query <- panc8_sub[, panc8_sub$tech == "fluidigmc1"] srt_ref <- integration_scop(   srt_ref,   batch = "tech",   integration_method = "Uncorrected" ) CellDimPlot(srt_ref, group.by = c("celltype", "tech"))  # Projection srt_query <- RunPCAMap(   srt_query = srt_query,   srt_ref = srt_ref,   ref_pca = "Uncorrectedpca",   ref_umap = "UncorrectedUMAP2D" ) ProjectionPlot(   srt_query = srt_query,   srt_ref = srt_ref,   query_group = "celltype",   ref_group = "celltype" )

title: Run PHATE (Potential of Heat-diffusion for Affinity-based Trajectory Embedding) — RunPHATE
code: RunPHATE(object, ...)  # S3 method for class 'Seurat' RunPHATE(   object,   reduction = "pca",   dims = NULL,   features = NULL,   assay = NULL,   layer = "data",   n_components = 2,   knn = 5,   decay = 40,   n_landmark = 2000,   t = "auto",   gamma = 1,   n_pca = 100,   knn_dist = "euclidean",   knn_max = NULL,   t_max = 100,   do_cluster = FALSE,   n_clusters = "auto",   max_clusters = 100,   reduction.name = "phate",   reduction.key = "PHATE_",   verbose = TRUE,   seed.use = 11,   ... )  # Default S3 method RunPHATE(   object,   assay = NULL,   n_components = 2,   knn = 5,   decay = 40,   n_landmark = 2000,   t = "auto",   gamma = 1,   n_pca = 100,   knn_dist = "euclidean",   knn_max = NULL,   t_max = 100,   do_cluster = FALSE,   n_clusters = "auto",   max_clusters = 100,   reduction.key = "PHATE_",   verbose = TRUE,   seed.use = 11,   ... )

title: Run PHATE (Potential of Heat-diffusion for Affinity-based Trajectory Embedding) — RunPHATE
text: object object. can Seurat object, matrix-like object, Neighbor object, Graph object. ... Additional arguments passed phate.PHATE. reduction dimensionality reduction use. Default "pca". dims dimensions used. Default NULL. features character vector features use. Default NULL. assay assay use. NULL, default assay Seurat object used. object also contains ChromatinAssay, default assay additional ChromatinAssay preprocessed sequentially. layer layer use. Default data. n_components number PHATE components. Default 2. knn number nearest neighbors build kernel. Default 5. decay sets decay rate kernel tails. Default 40. n_landmark number landmarks use fast PHATE. Default 2000. t power diffusion operator powered. sets level diffusion. "auto", t selected according knee point Von Neumann Entropy diffusion operator. Default "auto". gamma informational distance constant -1 1. gamma=1 gives PHATE log potential, gamma=0 gives square root potential. Default 1. n_pca number principal components use calculating neighborhoods. extremely large datasets, using n_pca < 20 allows neighborhoods calculated roughly log(n_samples) time. Default 100. knn_dist distance metric k-nearest neighbors. Recommended values: "euclidean", "cosine", "precomputed". Default "euclidean". knn_max maximum number neighbors alpha decaying kernel computed point. large datasets, setting knn_max small multiple knn can speed computation significantly. Default NULL. t_max maximum t test. Default 100. do_cluster Whether perform clustering PHATE embeddings. Default FALSE. n_clusters number clusters identified. Default "auto". max_clusters maximum number clusters test. Default 100. reduction.name name reduction stored Seurat object. Default "phate". reduction.key prefix column names PHATE embeddings. Default "PHATE_". verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE. seed.use Random seed reproducibility. Default 11.

title: Run PHATE (Potential of Heat-diffusion for Affinity-based Trajectory Embedding) — RunPHATE
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(pancreas_sub) pancreas_sub <- standard_scop(pancreas_sub) pancreas_sub <- RunPHATE(   object = pancreas_sub,   features = SeuratObject::VariableFeatures(pancreas_sub) ) CellDimPlot(   pancreas_sub,   group.by = "CellType",   reduction = "phate" ) } # }

title: Run PaCMAP (Pairwise Controlled Manifold Approximation) — RunPaCMAP
text: object object. can Seurat object, matrix-like object, Neighbor object, Graph object. ... Additional arguments passed pacmap.PaCMAP. reduction dimensionality reduction use. Default "pca". dims dimensions used. Default NULL. features character vector features use. Default NULL. assay assay use. NULL, default assay Seurat object used. object also contains ChromatinAssay, default assay additional ChromatinAssay preprocessed sequentially. layer layer use. Default data. n_components number PaCMAP components. Default 2. n.neighbors number neighbors considered k-Nearest Neighbor graph. Default 10 dataset whose sample size smaller 10000. large dataset whose sample size (n) larger 10000, default value : 10 + 15 * (log10(n) - 4). MN_ratio ratio ratio number mid-near pairs number neighbors. Default 0.5. FP_ratio ratio ratio number pairs number neighbors. Default 2. distance_method distance metric used. Default "euclidean". lr learning rate AdaGrad optimizer. Default 1. num_iters number iterations PaCMAP optimization. Default 450. apply_pca Whether pacmap apply PCA data constructing k-Nearest Neighbor graph. Using PCA preprocess data can largely accelerate DR process without losing much accuracy. Notice option affect initialization optimization process. Default TRUE. init initialization lower dimensional embedding. One "pca" "random". Default "random". reduction.name name reduction stored Seurat object. Default "pacmap". reduction.key prefix column names PaCMAP embeddings. Default "PaCMAP_". verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE. seed.use Random seed reproducibility. Default 11.

title: Run Palantir analysis — RunPalantir
code: data(pancreas_sub) pancreas_sub <- standard_scop(pancreas_sub) pancreas_sub <- RunPalantir(   pancreas_sub,   group.by = "SubCellType",   linear_reduction = "PCA",   nonlinear_reduction = "UMAP",   early_group = "Ductal",   terminal_groups = c("Alpha", "Beta", "Delta", "Epsilon") )  FeatureDimPlot(   pancreas_sub,   c("palantir_pseudotime", "palantir_diff_potential") )  FeatureDimPlot(   pancreas_sub,   grep(     "TerminalState_.*_diff_potential$",     colnames(pancreas_sub@meta.data),     value = TRUE   ) )  PalantirTrajectoryPlot(   pancreas_sub,   reduction = "UMAP",   pseudotime_interval = c(0, 0.9) )  PalantirTrajectoryPlot(   pancreas_sub,   reduction = "UMAP",   cell_color = "branch_selection",   pseudotime_interval = c(0, 0.9) )

title: Run RCTD spatial deconvolution — RunRCTD
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(scop)  data(visium_human_pancreas_sub) data(panc8_sub)  drop_celltypes <- c(   "epsilon",   "macrophage",   "mast",   "quiescent-stellate",   "schwann" )  panc8_rctd <- subset(   panc8_sub,   subset = !celltype %in% drop_celltypes )  table(panc8_sub$celltype) table(panc8_rctd$celltype)  spatial <- RunRCTD(   srt = visium_human_pancreas_sub,   reference = panc8_rctd,   reference_label = "celltype",   assay = "Spatial",   reference_assay = "RNA",   layer = "counts",   reference_layer = "counts",   rctd_mode = "full",   max_cores = 1,   prefix = "RCTD" )  rctd_cols <- grep("^RCTD_prop_", colnames(spatial@meta.data), value = TRUE) head(spatial@meta.data[, c("RCTD_dominant_type", "RCTD_max_prop", rctd_cols[1:3])])  SpatialSpotPlot(   spatial,   group.by = "RCTD_dominant_type",   theme_use = "theme_scop" )  SpatialSpotPlot(   spatial,   group.by = rctd_cols[1:min(4, length(rctd_cols))],   palette = "Spectral",   theme_use = "theme_scop" )  SpatialSpotPlot(   spatial,   group.by = "RCTD_dominant_type",   plot_type = "pie",   pie.radius.scale = 0.45,   theme_use = "theme_scop" ) } # }

title: Map query cells into a reference space — RunReferenceMapping
text: Reference mapping workflow modeled Seurat's MapQuery pattern. current implementation computes gene activity ATAC query cells, finds anchors RNA reference, integrates query reference embedding space, transfers labels, projects query cells reference UMAP.

title: Map query cells into a reference space — RunReferenceMapping

Default "ATAC". reference_assay Assay used reference object. reference_reduction Reduction used reference object. ref_umap UMAP reduction RNA reference used query projection. reference_dims Dimensions used reference reduction. reference_label Metadata column reference used transfer labels. label_method Label-transfer backend used anchor mapping. NULL, SCOP_SCOMM_PYTHON consulted reticulate defaults used otherwise. scomm_hidden_nodes, scomm_epochs, scomm_batch_size, scomm_threshold, scomm_seed Parameters passed optional scOMM backend. verbose Whether print message. title: Map query cells into a reference space — RunReferenceMapping
code: data("pbmcmultiome_sub", package = "scop") pbmcmultiome_sub <- standard_scop(   pbmcmultiome_sub,   assay = "RNA",   linear_reduction_dims = 20 ) reference <- subset(pbmcmultiome_sub, cells = colnames(pbmcmultiome_sub)[1:250]) query <- subset(pbmcmultiome_sub, cells = colnames(pbmcmultiome_sub)[251:350]) query <- standard_scop(   query,   assay = "peaks",   normalization_method = "TFIDF",   linear_reduction_dims = 20 ) query <- RunReferenceMapping(   srt = query,   reference = reference,   assay = "peaks",   reference_assay = "RNA",   reference_reduction = "Standardpca",   ref_umap = "StandardUMAP2D",   reference_label = "CellType",   reference_dims = 1:10,   dims = 2:10 )

title: Run SCENIC gene regulatory network analysis — RunSCENIC
text: srt Seurat object. assay assay use. NULL, default assay Seurat object used. object also contains ChromatinAssay, default assay additional ChromatinAssay preprocessed sequentially. layer Assay layer used count matrix. ranking_dbs Character vector cisTarget ranking feather files. NULL, gene-based v10 cisTarget ranking databases prepared species. motif_annotations Motif annotation table used scenic ctx. NULL, v10 motif2tf table prepared species. regulators Transcription factors used candidate regulators GRNBoost2. can character vector gene names one text file. NULL, cisTarget TF list prepared species. targets Optional target genes used restrict GRN output. can character vector gene names one text file. Regulator expression still kept predictor input GRNBoost2, adjacency table passed scenic ctx filtered targets. work_dir Directory used SCENIC input output files. species Species used select cisTarget reference files ranking_dbs, motif_annotations, regulators NULL. Supported values "Homo_sapiens", "Mus_musculus", "Drosophila_melanogaster". genome Genome build used select cisTarget reference files automatic references prepared. Human supports "hg38" (default) "hg19". Mouse fly currently use "mm10" "dm6", respectively. data_dir Directory used cache automatically prepared SCENIC reference files. NULL, files stored tools::R_user_dir("scop", "data")/SCENIC/<species>. prefix Prefix SCENIC output files. min_expr_cells Minimum number cells gene must detected GRNBoost2. run SCENIC metacells, first create metacell-level object RunMetaCell() pass object RunSCENIC(). min_regulon_size Minimum regulon size kept scenic ctx. include_negative_regulons Whether C++ backend also build negatively correlated regulons label TF(-). default matches pySCENIC's positive-regulon workflow labels C++ regulons TF(+). backend SCENIC backend. "cpp" uses R/C++ path "python" uses Python pySCENIC path. selected backend controls GRN, cisTarget pruning, AUCell scoring together. n_rounds Number boosting rounds used GRNBoost2. learning_rate Learning rate used GRNBoost2. max_depth Maximum tree depth used GRNBoost2. max_features Fraction features sampled GRNBoost2. subsample Row subsampling fraction used GRNBoost2. early_stop_window_length Early-stopping window used GRNBoost2. cores Number workers used GRNBoost2, scenic ctx, AUCell scoring. multicore execution supported, automatically reduced one core. seed Random seed used GRNBoost2 Seurat overclustering. force Whether rebuild existing SCENIC outputs. assay_name Name assay used store regulon activity scores. tool_name Name srt@tools entry. return_seurat Whether return modified Seurat object. FALSE, result list returned. envname Python environment used SCENIC. NULL, isolated "scenic_env" environment used. conda path command name conda-compatible executable. prepare_env Whether prepare configure SCENIC Python environment running. verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE.

title: Run SCENICPlus-style eGRN analysis — RunSCENICPlus

title: Run SCENICPlus-style eGRN analysis — RunSCENICPlus
code: RunSCENICPlus(   srt,   rna_assay = "RNA",   atac_assay = "peaks",   rna_layer = "counts",   atac_layer = "counts",   backend = c("cpp", "python"),   grn_method = c("grnboost2", "regdiffusion", "genie3"),   regulators = NULL,   rna_expr = NULL,   atac_expr = NULL,   tf_gene_prior = NULL,   region_gene_prior = NULL,   tf_region_prior = NULL,   triplets_prior = NULL,   eregulons_prior = NULL,   auc_expr = NULL,   auc_rankings = NULL,   region_gene_window = 250000,   region_gene_search_space = NULL,   region_gene_method = c("gbm", "correlation"),   region_gene_n_rounds = 500,   max_region_gene = 5,   max_tf_region = 500,   min_eregulon_size = 5,   egrn_rho_threshold = 0.05,   egrn_quantiles = c(0.85, 0.9, 0.95),   egrn_top_n_region_gene = c(5L, 10L, 15L),   egrn_min_target_genes = 10L,   group.by = NULL,   grn_top_targets = Inf,   max_grn_targets = Inf,   grn_target_scope = c("all", "region_gene"),   grn_n_rounds = 5000,   grn_learning_rate = 0.01,   grn_max_depth = 3,   grn_max_features = 0.1,   grn_subsample = 0.9,   grn_early_stop_window_length = 0,   seed = 666,   cores = 1,   assay_name = "scenicplus",   tool_name = "SCENICPlus",   python_result_dir = NULL,   scplus_object = NULL,   envname = "scenicplus_env",   conda = "auto",   prepare_env = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Run SCENICPlus-style eGRN analysis — RunSCENICPlus
text: srt Seurat object containing RNA chromatin assays. rna_assay RNA assay name. atac_assay Chromatin assay name. rna_layer RNA count layer used TF-gene inference AUC scoring. atac_layer ATAC count layer used peak-gene correlations. backend Runtime backend. "cpp" uses native package workflow. "python" checks official Python environment silently substitute native result. grn_method GRN method used TF-gene inference. native backend can call RunGRNBoost2() RunGENIE3(); RegDiffusion supported Python backend. regulators Candidate transcription factors. NULL, motif TF names used available; otherwise RNA genes considered. rna_expr Optional expression matrix used native TF-gene region-gene inference. Rows genes columns cells; cell--gene matrix accepted transposed cell names make orientation unambiguous. atac_expr Optional accessibility matrix used native region-gene inference. Rows regions columns cells; cell--region matrix accepted transposed cell names make orientation unambiguous. tf_gene_prior Optional precomputed TF-gene table columns TF, target, importance. supplied backend = "cpp", native GRN inference skipped table used TF-gene evidence layer. region_gene_prior Optional precomputed region-gene table columns region, gene, score. supplied backend = "cpp", native peak--gene correlation skipped. tf_region_prior Optional precomputed TF-region table columns TF, region, score. supplied backend = "cpp", existing motif incidence required. triplets_prior Optional precomputed TF-region-gene table columns TF, region, gene, score. supplied backend = "cpp", triplets read directly instead assembled three edge layers. eregulons_prior Optional precomputed eRegulon table columns regulon target. supplied backend = "cpp", eRegulon names gene sets taken table. auc_expr Optional expression matrix used eRegulon AUCell scoring native backend. Rows genes columns cells; cell--gene matrix accepted transposed cell names make orientation unambiguous. auc_rankings Optional precomputed 0-based ranking matrix used eRegulon AUCell scoring native backend. Rows cells columns genes; gene--cell matrix accepted transposed cell names make orientation unambiguous. mainly useful official SCENIC+ parity tests SCENIC+ uses seeded random tie-breaking. region_gene_window Maximum peak--gene distance base pairs. region_gene_search_space Optional search-space table columns region/Name gene/Gene. supplied, native region-gene scoring limited candidate pairs. region_gene_method Native region-gene scoring method. "gbm" follows official SCENIC+ region--gene strategy closely combining boosting feature importance Spearman correlation; "correlation" keeps older correlation-approximation. region_gene_n_rounds Number boosting rounds native region-gene importance scoring. max_region_gene Maximum region-gene links retained per gene. max_tf_region Maximum motif-supported regions retained per TF. min_eregulon_size Minimum genes per eRegulon retained AUC scoring. egrn_rho_threshold Absolute Spearman correlation threshold used split TF-gene region-gene links activating/repressing eGRN modules. egrn_quantiles Region-gene importance quantiles used build candidate eModules TF-gene leading-edge filtering. egrn_top_n_region_gene Top-N region-gene links per gene used additional candidate eModules TF-gene leading-edge filtering. egrn_min_target_genes Minimum leading-edge target genes required eRegulon module. default follows official SCENIC+ CLI workflow. group.Optional metadata column used calculate eRegulon specificity scores. grn_top_targets Maximum TF-gene edges retained per target. default Inf keeps positive links match arboreto GRNBoost2 output. max_grn_targets Maximum genes used TF-gene GRN targets native backend. grn_target_scope = "region_gene", genes ranked strongest absolute peak--gene correlation. Set Inf keep full selected target universe. grn_target_scope TF-gene target universe native backend. "" follows official SCENIC+ TF--gene inference scoring every RNA gene; "region_gene" restricts TF-gene inference genes peak links. grn_n_rounds Number native tree boosting rounds TF-gene inference. default follows arboreto SGBM_KWARGS. grn_learning_rate Native tree boosting learning rate. grn_max_depth Maximum depth native TF-gene regression tree. grn_max_features Fraction candidate TFs sampled native tree split. grn_subsample Fraction cells sampled native boosting round. grn_early_stop_window_length --bag improvement window used native GRNBoost2 early stopping. RunSCENICPlus default 0, disables early stopping match official SCENIC+ SGBM_KWARGS. seed Random seed used native stochastic boosting backends. default matches official SCENIC+ command-line wrappers. cores Number workers used native TF-gene GRN inference. assay_name Assay used store eRegulon AUC scores. tool_name Name srt@tools result entry. python_result_dir Directory containing official SCENIC+ outputs already exported tf_gene, region_gene, tf_region, triplets, eregulons, auc tables. Used backend = "python". scplus_object Optional official SCENIC+ Python object path (.pkl/.pickle) extract python_result_dir standardization. Used backend = "python". envname Python environment used backend = "python". conda Conda-compatible executable used backend = "python". prepare_env Whether prepare Python environment backend = "python". verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE.

title: Run SCExplorer — RunSCExplorer
text: base_dir base directory SCExplorer app. Default "SCExplorer". data_file name HDF5 file stores data matrices dataset. Default "data.hdf5". meta_file name HDF5 file stores metadata dataset. Default "meta.hdf5". title title SCExplorer app. Default "SCExplorer". initial_dataset initial dataset loaded app. Default NULL. initial_reduction initial dimensional reduction method loaded app. Default NULL. initial_group initial variable group cells app. Default NULL. initial_feature initial feature loaded app. Default NULL. initial_assay initial assay loaded app. Default NULL. initial_slot initial layer loaded app. Default NULL. initial_label Whether add labels initial plot. Default FALSE. initial_cell_palette initial color palette cells. Default "Chinese". initial_feature_palette initial color palette features. Default "Spectral". initial_theme initial theme plots. Default "theme_scop". initial_size initial size plots. Default 4. initial_ncol initial number columns arranging plots. Default 3. initial_arrange Whether use "Row" initial arrangement. Default TRUE. initial_raster Whether perform rasterization initial plot. default, set automatic, meaning TRUE number cells initial datasets exceeds 100,000. Default NULL. create_script Whether create SCExplorer app script. Default TRUE. style_script Whether style SCExplorer app script. Default TRUE. overwrite Whether overwrite existing data data file. Default TRUE. return_app Whether return SCExplorer app. Default TRUE. verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE.

title: Run SCExplorer — RunSCExplorer
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(pancreas_sub) pancreas_sub <- standard_scop(pancreas_sub) data(panc8_sub) panc8_sub <- integration_scop(   panc8_sub,   batch = "tech",   integration_method = "Harmony" ) panc8_sub <- standard_scop(panc8_sub)  PrepareSCExplorer(   list(     mouse_pancreas = pancreas_sub,     human_pancreas = panc8_sub   ),   base_dir = "./SCExplorer" )  # Create the app.R script app <- RunSCExplorer(   base_dir = "./SCExplorer",   initial_dataset = "mouse_pancreas",   initial_group = "CellType",   initial_feature = "Ncoa2" ) # Check files list.files("./SCExplorer")  # Run shiny app if (interactive()) {   check_r("shiny")   get_namespace_fun("shiny", "runApp")(app) } # Note: If scop installed in the isolated environment using renv, # add `renv::activate(project = "path/to/scop_env")` to the app.R script.   # You can deploy the app on the self-hosted shiny server # (https://www.rstudio.com/products/shiny/shiny-server/). # Or deploy the app on the website # (https://www.shinyapps.io) for free:  # step1: install "rsconnect" package and authorize your account # install.packages("rsconnect") # library(rsconnect) # setAccountInfo( #   name = "<NAME>", #   token = "<TOKEN>", #   secret = "<SECRET>" # )  ### step2: deploy the app # deployApp("./SCExplorer") } # }

title: Run scVelo workflow — RunSCVELO
text: scVelo scalable toolkit RNA velocity analysis single cells. function runs enhanced scVelo workflow Seurat object improved error handling, version compatibility, modular design.

title: Run scVelo workflow — RunSCVELO
code: RunSCVELO(   srt = NULL,   adata = NULL,   assay_x = "RNA",   layer_x = "counts",   assay_y = c("spliced", "unspliced"),   layer_y = "counts",   group.by = NULL,   linear_reduction = NULL,   nonlinear_reduction = NULL,   basis = NULL,   mode = "stochastic",   fitting_by = "stochastic",   magic_impute = FALSE,   knn = 5,   t = 2,   min_shared_counts = 30,   n_pcs = 30,   n_neighbors = 30,   filter_genes = TRUE,   min_counts = 3,   min_counts_u = 3,   normalize_per_cell = TRUE,   log_transform = TRUE,   use_raw = FALSE,   diff_kinetics = FALSE,   stream_smooth = NULL,   stream_density = 2,   arrow_length = 5,   arrow_size = 5,   arrow_density = 0.5,   denoise = FALSE,   denoise_topn = 3,   kinetics = FALSE,   kinetics_topn = 100,   calculate_velocity_genes = FALSE,   compute_velocity_confidence = TRUE,   compute_velocity_graph = NULL,   compute_terminal_states = FALSE,   compute_pseudotime = FALSE,   compute_paga = FALSE,   top_n = 6,   cores = 1,   palette = "Chinese",   palcolor = NULL,   legend.position = "on data",   show_plot = TRUE,   save_plot = FALSE,   plot_format = c("pdf", "png", "svg"),   plot_dpi = 300,   plot_prefix = "scvelo",   dirpath = "./scvelo",   backend = c("python", "cpp"),   return_seurat = !is.null(srt),   verbose = TRUE )

title: Run scVelo workflow — RunSCVELO
text: srt Seurat object. Default NULL. provided, adata ignored. adata anndata object. Default NULL. assay_x Assay convert main data matrix anndata object. Default "RNA". layer_x Layer name assay_x Seurat object. Default "counts". assay_y Assays convert layers anndata object. Default c("spliced", "unspliced"). layer_y Layer names assay_y Seurat object. Default "counts". group.Name one meta.data columns group (color) cells . linear_reduction linear dimensionality reduction method use. Options "pca", "svd", "ica", "nmf", "mds", "glmpca". Default "pca". nonlinear_reduction nonlinear dimensionality reduction method use. Options "umap", "umap-naive", "tsne", "dm", "phate", "pacmap", "trimap", "largevis", "fr". Default "umap". basis basis use reduction, e.g., "UMAP". mode Velocity estimation models use. Can "deterministic", "stochastic", "dynamical". fitting_by Method used fit gene velocities dynamical modeling. Default "stochastic". magic_impute Flag indicating whether perform magic imputation. Default FALSE. knn number nearest neighbors magic.MAGIC. Default 5. t Power diffusion operator powered magic.MAGIC. Default 2. min_shared_counts Minimum number counts (unspliced spliced) required gene. Default 30. n_pcs Number principal components use linear reduction. Default 30. n_neighbors Number neighbors use constructing KNN graph. Default 30. filter_genes Whether filter genes based minimum counts. min_counts Minimum counts gene filtering. min_counts_u Minimum unspliced counts gene filtering. normalize_per_cell Whether normalize counts per cell. log_transform Whether apply log transformation. use_raw Whether use raw data dynamical modeling. diff_kinetics Whether use differential kinetics. stream_smooth Multiplication factor scale Gaussian kernel around grid point. stream_density Controls closeness streamlines. density = 2 (default), domain divided 60x60 grid, whereas density linearly scales grid. cell grid can , , one traversing streamline. Default 2. arrow_length Length arrows. arrow_size Size arrows. Default 5. arrow_density Amount velocities show. denoise Boolean flag indicating whether denoise. denoise_topn Number genes highest likelihood selected infer velocity directions. kinetics Boolean flag indicating whether estimate RNA kinetics. kinetics_topn Number genes highest likelihood selected infer velocity directions. calculate_velocity_genes Boolean flag indicating whether calculate velocity genes. compute_velocity_confidence Whether compute velocity confidence metrics. compute_velocity_graph Whether compute store velocity graph downstream terminal-state pseudotime calculations. NULL, compute graph terminal states pseudotime requested. compute_terminal_states Whether compute terminal states (root end points). compute_pseudotime Whether compute velocity pseudotime. compute_paga Whether compute PAGA (Partition-based graph abstraction). top_n number top features plot. cores number cores use cellrank. palette Color palette name. Available palettes can found thisplot::show_palettes. Default "Chinese". palcolor Custom colors used create color palette. Default NULL. legend.position Position legend plots. Can "data", "right margin", "bottom right", etc. Default "data". show_plot Whether show plot. Default FALSE. save_plot Whether save plots files. Default FALSE. plot_format Format saved plots: "png" (default), "pdf", "svg". plot_dpi Resolution (DPI) saved plots. Default 300. plot_prefix Prefix saved plot filenames. Default "cellrank". dirpath directory save plots. Default "./cellrank". backend Backend used compute RNA velocity. "python" keeps original scVelo workflow. "cpp" uses package C++ implementation stochastic velocity embedding compatible VelocityPlot. return_seurat Whether return Seurat object instead anndata object. Default TRUE. verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE.

title: Run scVelo workflow — RunSCVELO
code: data(pancreas_sub) pancreas_sub <- standard_scop(pancreas_sub) pancreas_sub <- RunSCVELO(   pancreas_sub,   assay_x = "RNA",   group.by = "SubCellType",   linear_reduction = "PCA",   nonlinear_reduction = "UMAP",   backend = "cpp" )  FeatureDimPlot(   pancreas_sub,   c(     "stochastic_length",     "stochastic_confidence"   ) )  VelocityPlot(   pancreas_sub,   reduction = "UMAP",   plot_type = "stream" )  CellDimPlot(   pancreas_sub,   group.by = "SubCellType",   reduction = "UMAP",   pt.size = NA,   velocity = "stochastic" )

title: Run scTenifoldKnk in-silico knockout analysis — RunscTenifoldKnk
code: data(pancreas_sub) gene_use <- "Pdx1" counts <- GetAssayData5(   pancreas_sub,   assay = "RNA",   layer = "counts" ) detected <- names(   sort(Matrix::rowSums(counts > 0),     decreasing = TRUE   ) ) features_use <- unique(c(gene_use, head(detected, 300)))  pancreas_sub <- RunscTenifoldKnk(   pancreas_sub,   gKO = gene_use,   features = features_use,   qc = FALSE,   nc_nNet = 3,   nc_nCells = 200,   td_maxIter = 200,   store_networks = FALSE,   store_manifold = TRUE )  dr <- pancreas_sub@tools$scTenifoldKnk$diffRegulation head(dr)  scTenifoldKnkPlot(pancreas_sub, plot_type = "effect")

title: Single-cell reference mapping with Seurat method — RunSeuratMap
code: RunSeuratMap(   srt_query,   srt_ref,   query_assay = NULL,   ref_pca = NULL,   ref_assay = srt_ref[[ref_pca]]@assay.used,   ref_dims = 1:30,   ref_umap = NULL,   ref_group = NULL,   normalization.method = "LogNormalize",   reduction_project_method = "pcaproject",   k.anchor = 5,   k.filter = 200,   k.score = 30,   k.weight = 100,   projection_method = c("model", "knn"),   nn_method = NULL,   k = 30,   distance_metric = "cosine",   vote_fun = "mean",   verbose = TRUE )

title: Single-cell reference mapping with Seurat method — RunSeuratMap
text: srt_query object class Seurat annotated cell types. srt_ref Seurat object count matrix representing reference object. provided, similarities calculated cells query reference objects. provided, similarities calculated within query object. query_assay assay use query object. provided, default assay query object used. ref_pca character string specifying name PCA reduction reference object use calculating distance metric. ref_assay assay use reference object. provided, default assay reference object used. ref_dims numeric vector specifying dimension indices reference reduction used calculating distance metric. ref_umap character string specifying name UMAP reduction reference object. provided, first UMAP reduction found reference object used. ref_group grouping variable reference object. variable used group cells heatmap columns. provided, cells treated one group. normalization.method normalization method use. Default `"LogNormalize"`. reduction_project_method Dimensional reduction perform finding anchors. Default `"pcaproject"`. k.anchor many neighbors (k) use finding anchors. Default `5`. k.filter many neighbors (k) use filtering anchors. Set NA turn filtering. Default `200`. k.score many neighbors (k) use scoring anchors. Default `30`. k.weight Number neighbors consider weighting anchors. Default `100`. projection_method character string specifying projection method use. Options "model" "knn". "model" selected, function try use pre-trained UMAP model reference object projection. "knn" selected, function directly find nearest neighbors using distance metric. nn_method character string specifying nearest neighbor search method use. Options "raw", "annoy", "rann". "raw" selected, function use brute-force method find nearest neighbors. "annoy" selected, function use Annoy library approximate nearest neighbor search. "rann" selected, function use RANN library approximate nearest neighbor search. provided, function choose search method based size query reference datasets. k number nearest neighbors find cell query object. distance_metric distance metric use calculating pairwise distances cells. Options include: "pearson", "spearman", "cosine", "correlation", "jaccard", "ejaccard", "dice", "edice", "hamman", "simple matching", "faith". Additional distance metrics can also used, "euclidean", "manhattan", "hamming", etc. vote_fun character string specifying function used aggregating nearest neighbors reference object. Options "mean", "median", "sum", "min", "max", "sd", "var", etc. provided, default "mean". verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE.

title: Single-cell reference mapping with Seurat method — RunSeuratMap
code: data(panc8_sub) panc8_sub <- standard_scop(panc8_sub) srt_ref <- panc8_sub[, panc8_sub$tech != "fluidigmc1"] srt_query <- panc8_sub[, panc8_sub$tech == "fluidigmc1"] srt_ref <- integration_scop(   srt_ref,   batch = "tech",   integration_method = "Uncorrected" ) CellDimPlot(srt_ref, group.by = c("celltype", "tech"))  # Projection srt_query <- RunSeuratMap(   srt_query = srt_query,   srt_ref = srt_ref,   ref_pca = "Uncorrectedpca",   ref_umap = "UncorrectedUMAP2D",   k.weight = 50 ) ProjectionPlot(   srt_query = srt_query,   srt_ref = srt_ref,   query_group = "celltype",   ref_group = "celltype" )

Use NULL avoid writing single-sample result files working directory. outdir Output directory passed multi-sample SpatialEcoTyper. NULL creates temporary directory. radius Spatial neighborhood radius, units x.y.. resolution Louvain clustering resolution used SpatialEcoTyper. nfeatures Number variable features used SpatialEcoTyper. min.cts.per.region Minimum number cell types required spatial neighborhood. npcs Number principal components used similarity networks. min.cells Minimum number cells spatial meta-cells expressing feature. min.features Minimum number features detected cell spatial meta-cell. iterations Number similarity network fusion iterations. minibatch Number columns processed per mini-batch SNF. ncores Number CPU cores used SpatialEcoTyper. grid.size Spatial grid size used discretize coordinates. filter.region..celltypes Optional cell types used restrict spatial neighborhoods. k Number spatial nearest neighbors used construct spatial meta-cells. k.sn Number nearest neighbors used construct similarity networks. dropcell Whether cells without spatial ecotype assignments removed returned SpatialEcoTyper metadata. normalization.method, nmf_ranks, nrun.per.rank, min.coph, Region, downsample..region, subresolution, seed Parameters passed SpatialEcoTyper::MultiSpatialEcoTyper(). scale Whether scale expression "recover" "deconvolute". Ws Pretrained basis matrices passed SpatialEcoTyper::RecoverSE(). ncell.per.run Number cells processed per run SpatialEcoTyper::RecoverSE(). min.score Minimum prediction score passed SpatialEcoTyper::RecoverSE(). W Pretrained basis matrix passed SpatialEcoTyper::DeconvoluteSE(). nsample.per.run Number samples processed per run SpatialEcoTyper::DeconvoluteSE(). sum2one Whether inferred SE abundances normalized sum one. prefix Prefix used output metadata columns. tool_name Name used store detailed results srt@tools. store_results Whether store raw results srt@tools. allow_partial Whether allow missing SE labels cells absent returned SpatialEcoTyper metadata. code: RunSpotQC(   srt,   assay = NULL,   return_filtered = FALSE,   qc_metrics = c("outlier", "umi", "gene", "mito"),   outlier_threshold = c("log10_nCount:lower:3", "log10_nFeature:lower:3",     "spot_featurecount_dist:lower:3"),   outlier_n = 1,   UMI_threshold = 500,   gene_threshold = 200,   mito_threshold = 20,   mito_pattern = c("MT-", "Mt-", "mt-"),   mito_gene = NULL,   verbose = TRUE,   seed = 11 )

title: Run spot-level quality control — RunSpotQC
text: srt Seurat object. assay assay use. NULL, default assay Seurat object used. object also contains ChromatinAssay, default assay additional ChromatinAssay preprocessed sequentially. return_filtered Logical indicating whether return spot-filtered Seurat object. Default FALSE. qc_metrics QC metrics apply. Available metrics "outlier", "umi", "gene", "mito". outlier_threshold Character vector specifying outlier thresholds "metric:direction:nmads". Available default metrics "log10_nCount", "log10_nFeature", "spot_featurecount_dist". outlier_n Minimum number outlier metrics required fail spot. UMI_threshold Minimum UMI count required pass "umi" QC. gene_threshold Minimum detected gene count required pass "gene" QC. mito_threshold Maximum mitochondrial percentage allowed "mito" QC. mito_pattern Regex patterns used identify mitochondrial genes. mito_gene Optional explicit mitochondrial gene vector. provided, mito_pattern ignored. verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE. seed Random seed reproducibility.

what: Single-cell reference mapping with Symphony method — RunSymphonyMap
title: Single-cell reference mapping with Symphony method — RunSymphonyMap
text: Single-cell reference mapping Symphony method

title: Single-cell reference mapping with Symphony method — RunSymphonyMap
code: RunSymphonyMap(   srt_query,   srt_ref,   query_assay = NULL,   ref_assay = srt_ref[[ref_pca]]@assay.used,   ref_pca = NULL,   ref_harmony = NULL,   ref_umap = NULL,   ref_group = NULL,   projection_method = c("model", "knn"),   nn_method = NULL,   k = 30,   distance_metric = "cosine",   vote_fun = "mean",   verbose = TRUE )

title: Single-cell reference mapping with Symphony method — RunSymphonyMap
text: srt_query object class Seurat annotated cell types. srt_ref Seurat object count matrix representing reference object. provided, similarities calculated cells query reference objects. provided, similarities calculated within query object. query_assay assay use query object. provided, default assay query object used. ref_assay assay use reference object. provided, default assay reference object used. ref_pca PCA reduction reference object use calculating distance metric. ref_harmony Harmony reduction reference object use calculating distance metric. ref_umap character string specifying name UMAP reduction reference object. provided, first UMAP reduction found reference object used. ref_group grouping variable reference object. variable used group cells heatmap columns. provided, cells treated one group. projection_method character string specifying projection method use. Options "model" "knn". "model" selected, function try use pre-trained UMAP model reference object projection. "knn" selected, function directly find nearest neighbors using distance metric. nn_method character string specifying nearest neighbor search method use. Options "raw", "annoy", "rann". "raw" selected, function use brute-force method find nearest neighbors. "annoy" selected, function use Annoy library approximate nearest neighbor search. "rann" selected, function use RANN library approximate nearest neighbor search. provided, function choose search method based size query reference datasets. k number nearest neighbors find cell query object. distance_metric distance metric use calculating pairwise distances cells. Options include: "pearson", "spearman", "cosine", "correlation", "jaccard", "ejaccard", "dice", "edice", "hamman", "simple matching", "faith". Additional distance metrics can also used, "euclidean", "manhattan", "hamming", etc. vote_fun character string specifying function used aggregating nearest neighbors reference object. Options "mean", "median", "sum", "min", "max", "sd", "var", etc. provided, default "mean". verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE.

title: Single-cell reference mapping with Symphony method — RunSymphonyMap
code: data(panc8_sub) panc8_sub <- standard_scop(panc8_sub) srt_ref <- panc8_sub[, panc8_sub$tech != "fluidigmc1"] srt_query <- panc8_sub[, panc8_sub$tech == "fluidigmc1"] srt_ref <- integration_scop(   srt_ref,   batch = "tech",   integration_method = "Harmony" ) CellDimPlot(srt_ref, group.by = c("celltype", "tech"))  # Projection srt_query <- RunSymphonyMap(   srt_query = srt_query,   srt_ref = srt_ref,   ref_pca = "Harmonypca",   ref_harmony = "Harmony",   ref_umap = "HarmonyUMAP2D" ) ProjectionPlot(   srt_query = srt_query,   srt_ref = srt_ref,   query_group = "celltype",   ref_group = "celltype" )

code: RunTriMap(object, ...)  # S3 method for class 'Seurat' RunTriMap(   object,   reduction = "pca",   dims = NULL,   features = NULL,   assay = NULL,   layer = "data",   n_components = 2,   n_inliers = 12,   n_outliers = 4,   n_random = 3,   distance_method = "euclidean",   lr = 0.1,   n_iters = 400,   apply_pca = TRUE,   opt_method = "dbd",   reduction.name = "trimap",   reduction.key = "TriMap_",   verbose = TRUE,   seed.use = 11L,   ... )  # Default S3 method RunTriMap(   object,   assay = NULL,   n_components = 2,   n_inliers = 12,   n_outliers = 4,   n_random = 3,   distance_method = "euclidean",   lr = 0.1,   n_iters = 400,   apply_pca = TRUE,   opt_method = "dbd",   reduction.key = "TriMap_",   verbose = TRUE,   seed.use = 11L,   ... )

title: Run TriMap (Large-scale Dimensionality Reduction Using Triplets) — RunTriMap
text: object object. can Seurat object, matrix-like object, Neighbor object, Graph object. ... Additional arguments passed trimap.TRIMAP function. reduction dimensionality reduction use. Default "pca". dims dimensions used. Default NULL. features character vector features use. Default NULL. assay assay use. NULL, default assay Seurat object used. object also contains ChromatinAssay, default assay additional ChromatinAssay preprocessed sequentially. layer layer use. Default data. n_components number TriMap components. Default 2. n_inliers number nearest neighbors forming nearest neighbor triplets. Default 12. n_outliers number outliers forming nearest neighbor triplets. Default 4. n_random number random triplets per point. Default 3. distance_method character string specifying distance metric TriMap. Options : "euclidean", "manhattan", "angular", "cosine", "hamming". Default "euclidean". lr learning rate TriMap. Default 0.1. n_iters number iterations TriMap. Default 400. apply_pca Whether apply PCA nearest-neighbor calculation. Default TRUE. opt_method character string specifying optimization method TriMap. Options : "dbd", "sd", "momentum". Default "dbd". reduction.name character string specifying name reduction stored Seurat object. Default "trimap". reduction.key character string specifying prefix column names TriMap embeddings. Default "TriMap_". verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE. seed.use Random seed reproducibility. Default 11.

title: Run TriMap (Large-scale Dimensionality Reduction Using Triplets) — RunTriMap
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(pancreas_sub) pancreas_sub <- standard_scop(pancreas_sub) pancreas_sub <- RunTriMap(   object = pancreas_sub,   features = SeuratObject::VariableFeatures(pancreas_sub) ) CellDimPlot(   pancreas_sub,   group.by = "CellType",   reduction = "trimap" ) } # }

title: Run UMAP (Uniform Manifold Approximation and Projection) — RunUMAP2
text: object object. can Seurat object, matrix-like object, Neighbor object, Graph object. ... Additional arguments passed UMAP. reduction dimensionality reduction use. Default "pca". dims dimensions used. Default NULL. features character vector features use. Default NULL. neighbor name Neighbor object used. Default NULL. graph name Graph object used. Default NULL. assay assay use. NULL, default assay Seurat object used. object also contains ChromatinAssay, default assay additional ChromatinAssay preprocessed sequentially. layer layer use. Default data. umap.method UMAP method used. Options "naive" "uwot". Default "uwot". reduction.model DimReduc object containing pre-trained UMAP model. Default NULL. n_threads Num threads used. return.model Whether return UMAP model. Default FALSE. n.neighbors number nearest neighbors used. Default 30. n.components number UMAP components. Default 2. metric metric function used distance calculations. using string, available metrics : euclidean, manhattan. available generalized metrics : cosine, pearson, pearson2. Note triangle inequality may satisfied generalized metrics, hence knn search may optimal. using metric.function function, signature must function(matrix, origin, target) compute distance origin column target columns. Default "cosine". n.epochs number iterations performed layout optimization UMAP. Default 200. cores spread parameter UMAP, used automatic estimation /b parameters. Default 1. min.dist minimum distance UMAP embeddings, determines close points appear final layout. Default 0.3. set.op.mix.ratio Interpolate (fuzzy) union intersection set operation used combine local fuzzy simplicial sets obtain global fuzzy simplicial sets. fuzzy set operations use product t-norm. value parameter 0.0 1.0; value 1.0 use pure fuzzy union, 0.0 use pure fuzzy intersection. local.connectivity local connectivity, used construction fuzzy simplicial set. Default 1. negative.sample.rate negative sample rate UMAP optimization. Determines many non-neighbor points used per point per iteration layout optimization. Default 5. parameter UMAP optimization. Contributes gradient calculations layout optimization. left NA, suitable value estimated automatically. Default NULL. b parameter b UMAP optimization. Details see parameter . learning.rate initial value "learning rate" layout optimization. Default 1. repulsion.strength numeric value determines, together alpha, learning rate layout optimization. Default 1. reduction.name name reduction stored Seurat object. Default "umap". reduction.key prefix column names UMAP embeddings. Default "UMAP_". verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE. seed.use Random seed reproducibility. Default 11.

title: Run UMAP (Uniform Manifold Approximation and Projection) — RunUMAP2
code: data(pancreas_sub) pancreas_sub <- standard_scop(pancreas_sub) pancreas_sub <- RunUMAP2(pancreas_sub, dims = 1:30) CellDimPlot(   pancreas_sub,   group.by = "CellType",   reduction = "umap" )

text: srt Seurat object. Default NULL. provided, adata ignored. assay_x Assay convert main data matrix anndata object. Default "RNA". layer_x Layer name assay_x Seurat object. Default "counts". assay_y Assays convert layers anndata object. Default c("spliced", "unspliced"). layer_y Layer names assay_y Seurat object. Default "counts". adata anndata object. Default NULL. group.Name one meta.data columns group (color) cells . time_field character string specifying column name adata.obs srt@meta.data contains time information. growth_iters number growth iterations perform OT Model computation. Default 3. tmap_out character string specifying path store computed transport maps. time_from starting time point trajectory fate analysis. time_to ending time point trajectory fate analysis. provided, trajectory fate analysis specified time_from performed. get_coupling Whether compute store coupling matrix specified time_from time_to. Default FALSE. recalculate Whether recalculate transport maps even already exist specified tmap_out location. Default FALSE. palette Color palette name. Available palettes can found thisplot::show_palettes. Default "Chinese". palcolor Custom colors used create color palette. Default NULL. show_plot Whether show plot. Default FALSE. save_plot Whether save plots files. Default FALSE. plot_format Format saved plots: "png" (default), "pdf", "svg". plot_dpi Resolution (DPI) saved plots. Default 300. plot_prefix Prefix saved plot filenames. Default "cellrank". dirpath directory save plots. Default "./cellrank". return_seurat Whether return Seurat object instead anndata object. Default TRUE. verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE.

title: Run WOT analysis — RunWOT
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(pancreas_sub) pancreas_sub <- standard_scop(pancreas_sub) pancreas_sub <- RunSlingshot(   pancreas_sub,   group.by = "SubCellType",   reduction = "UMAP" )  print(range(pancreas_sub$Lineage1, na.rm = TRUE))  pancreas_sub <- RunWOT(   pancreas_sub,   group.by = "SubCellType",   time_field = "Lineage1",   time_from = min(pancreas_sub$Lineage1, na.rm = TRUE),   time_to = max(pancreas_sub$Lineage1, na.rm = TRUE),   get_coupling = TRUE,   tmap_out = "tmaps/lineage_tmap" )  pancreas_sub$Custom_Time <- sample(   1:10,   ncol(pancreas_sub),   replace = TRUE ) pancreas_sub <- RunWOT(   pancreas_sub,   group.by = "CellType",   time_field = "Custom_Time",   time_from = 1,   time_to = 10,   tmap_out = "tmaps/custom_tmap" ) } # }

title: Run mcRigor metacell partition assessment — RunmcRigor
text: srt Seurat object containing original single-cell data. cell_membership data frame matrix cells rows one metacell partitions columns. Row names cell names. row names missing row count equals ncol(srt), cells matched colnames(srt) order. metacell.Metadata column(s) srt used metacell partitions cell_membership = NULL. mode mcRigor task. "detect" calls mcRigor_DETECT() one partition; "optimize" calls mcRigor_OPTIMIZE() across candidate partitions. tgamma Target partition/gamma "detect". Can membership column name numeric gamma label used mcRigor. NULL, first membership column used. gamma_names Optional gamma labels membership columns. mcRigor requires numeric-like column labels; non-numeric labels mapped internally 1:ncol(cell_membership) recorded stored result. assay_type Assay type passed mcRigor. Gammas Candidate gamma labels "optimize". Can use original membership column names mapped mcRigor gamma labels. aggregate_method Metacell aggregation method passed mcRigor. output_file Optional path mcRigor writes TabMC RDS file. NULL, temporary file used avoid creating files working directory. Nrep Number permutation repetitions used mcRigor. gene_filter, feature_use, cor_method, prePro, test_cutoff, thre_smooth, thre_bw Parameters forwarded mcRigor. D_bw, optim_method, weight, dub_rate Optimization parameters forwarded mcRigor_OPTIMIZE(). draw, pur_metric, check_purity, fields, step_save Plotting, purity, intermediate-save parameters forwarded mcRigor. prefix Prefix metadata columns written srt. tool_name Name srt@tools entry used store results. verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE.

title: Run mcRigor metacell partition assessment — RunmcRigor

title: Run mcRigor metacell partition assessment — RunmcRigor
text: Liu, P. Li, J.J. (2024). mcRigor: statistical method enhance rigor metacell partitioning single-cell data analysis. bioRxiv. doi:10.1101/2024.10.30.621093

title: Run mcRigor metacell partition assessment — RunmcRigor
code: data(pancreas_sub) set.seed(11) pancreas_sub <- standard_scop(   pancreas_sub,   nHVF = 500,   linear_reduction_dims = 20,   linear_reduction_dims_use = 1:20,   nonlinear_reduction_dims = 2,   verbose = FALSE ) mc <- RunMetaCell(   pancreas_sub,   method = "supercell",   gamma = 25 )  membership <- data.frame(   Metacell = mc@misc[["cell_membership"]],   row.names = names(mc@misc[["cell_membership"]]) )  pancreas_sub <- RunmcRigor(   mc@misc[["original_srt"]],   cell_membership = membership,   Nrep = 1,   feature_use = 100,   draw = FALSE )  table(pancreas_sub$mcRigor_status)  CellDimPlot(   pancreas_sub,   group.by = "mcRigor_metacell" )  CellDimPlot(   pancreas_sub,   group.by = "mcRigor_status" )

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data("pbmcmultiome_sub", package = "scop") pbmcmultiome_sub <- standard_scop(pbmcmultiome_sub, assay = "RNA") ref_cells <- colnames(pbmcmultiome_sub)[1:250] query_cells <- colnames(pbmcmultiome_sub)[251:500] reference <- subset(pbmcmultiome_sub, cells = ref_cells) query <- subset(pbmcmultiome_sub, cells = query_cells) query <- RunscOMM(   srt = query,   reference = reference,   reference_assay = "RNA",   query_assay = "RNA",   reference_label = "CellType",   scomm_epochs = 1 ) CellDimPlot(   query,   group.by = c(     "CellType",     "scomm_prediction"   ),   xlab = "UMAP_1",   ylab = "UMAP_2" )  FeatureDimPlot(   query,   features = c(     "scomm_score.B",     "scomm_score.max"   ),   xlab = "UMAP_1",   ylab = "UMAP_2" ) } # }

code: data(pancreas_sub) counts <- GetAssayData5(pancreas_sub, assay = "RNA", layer = "counts") detected <- names(sort(Matrix::rowSums(counts > 0), decreasing = TRUE)) features_use <- head(detected, 300)  pancreas_sub <- RunscTenifoldNet(   pancreas_sub,   group.by = "CellType",   condition1 = "Ductal",   condition2 = "Endocrine",   features = features_use,   qc = FALSE,   nc_nNet = 3,   nc_nCells = 200,   td_maxIter = 200,   ma_nDim = 2,   store_networks = FALSE,   store_manifold = TRUE )  dr <- pancreas_sub@tools$scTenifoldNet$diffRegulation head(dr)  scTenifoldNetPlot(pancreas_sub, plot_type = "effect")

title: Run tAge transcriptomic aging-clock prediction — RuntAge
text: object Seurat object, ExpressionSet, expression matrix-like object genes rows pseudobulk/bulk samples columns. model_paths Named list character vector local tAge model files (.rds backend = "cpp", .pkl backend = "python"). Names must match one "scaled", "scaled_diff", "yugene", "yugene_diff". NULL, model files downloaded mengxu98/datasets C++ backend Zenodo Python backend. backend Model prediction backend. "cpp" uses converted Elastic Net models C++ prediction. "python" uses tAge::predict_tAge(). clock tAge clock family used model_paths = NULL. model_species Model species scope used model_paths = NULL. "auto" chooses "Mouse" species = "mouse", "Rodents" species = "rat", "Multispecies" otherwise. model_tissue Model tissue scope used model_paths = NULL. model_preprocessing Preprocessing-specific model file(s) used model_paths = NULL. Values map tAge model filename suffixes. model_cache_dir Directory used cache downloaded tAge models. NULL, uses tools::R_user_dir("scop", "data"). datasets_base_url Base URL local directory containing converted tAge EN models mengxu98/datasets. zenodo_record Zenodo record ID tAge model files. max_model_size Maximum model file size, bytes, allowed automatic download. Increase use Inf large Bayesian Ridge models. metadata Sample metadata matrix input. Row names must match columns object. NULL, minimal sample_id metadata created. group.Metadata columns used form pseudobulk groups Seurat input. NULL, cells aggregated sequentially across whole object. split.control_group_column automatically included aggregation metadata provided. split.Optional pseudobulk metadata column used run tAge separately group, tissue. assay, layer Assay layer used raw counts Seurat input. species Species passed tAge. Supported values "mouse", "human", "rat", "monkey". mode tAge model mode: "EN" Elastic Net "BR" Bayesian Ridge. gene_mapping_type Gene identifier type passed tAge preprocessing: "Gene.Symbol" "Ensembl". coverage_threshold Minimum cumulative read count per pseudobulk sample Seurat input. count_threshold, percent_threshold Gene filtering thresholds passed tAge::tAge_preprocessing(). control_group_column, control_group_label Optional control group used tAge control subtraction. remove_outliers Whether run tAge::remove_outliers() pseudobulk ExpressionSet. outlier.Optional metadata column used split samples outlier detection. Defaults split.. outlier_n_components, outlier_threshold_quantile, outlier_min_samples Parameters passed tAge::remove_outliers(). min_samples Minimum pseudobulk samples per split.group tAge::tAge_by_group(). shuffle, seed Whether shuffle cells pseudobulk aggregation random seed used shuffle. check_python Whether verify active reticulate Python can import joblib, pandas, sklearn tAge prediction. tool_name Name Seurat tool entry used store results. store_eset, store_processed Whether store pseudobulk ExpressionSet processed tAge ExpressionSet list returned Seurat tool entry list result. verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE.

title: Plot top regulon specificity scores from SCENIC results — SCENICPlot
text: srt Seurat object containing SCENIC results RunSCENIC(). group.Metadata column used cell group annotation. tool_name Name srt@tools entry storing SCENIC results. assay Assay used fallback source regulon activity. layer Assay layer used fallback source regulon activity. plot_type Plot type. "rss_rank" keeps original regulon RSS rank plot. options summarize RSS, regulon activity, regulon sizes, TF-target subnetworks. features Optional TF/regulon names used activity, network, target plots. Values can match either "Sox9" "Sox9(+)". Explicit values resolved input order; duplicated regulons drawn . reduction Dimensional reduction used plot_type = "activity_dim". NULL, UMAP/tSNE/PCA-like reduction selected available. dims Two reduction dimensions used plot_type = "activity_dim". cor.features Two metadata columns gene names used plot_type = "activity_cor_dumbbell". SCENIC regulon activity correlated feature across matched cells. cor.feature.labels Optional labels cor.features plot legend. NULL, cor.features used. cor_method Correlation method used plot_type = "activity_cor_dumbbell". p_cutoff P-value cutoff used mark significant correlations plot_type = "activity_cor_dumbbell". cor_sort.Ordering used dumbbell rows. "difference" sorts absolute distance two correlations, "max_abs" sorts strongest absolute correlation, "input" keeps resolved regulon order. cor_cols Two colors used two correlation targets. cor_xlim Optional x-axis limits dumbbell plot. cor_label Whether label dumbbell points correlation coefficients. top_n Number top regulons labeled group. activity_scale Whether z-score regulon across groups plot_type = "activity_heatmap". default FALSE heatmap shows mean regulon activity collapse constant regulons zero. rss_scale Whether z-score regulon across groups plot_type = "rss_heatmap". Use rss_scale = TRUE, activity_scale = TRUE, heatmap_limits value RSS activity heatmaps use comparable row-wise relative scale. heatmap_show_row_names, heatmap_show_column_names Whether show row column names plot_type = "rss_heatmap" plot_type = "activity_heatmap". heatmap_cluster_rows, heatmap_cluster_columns Whether cluster rows columns SCENIC heatmaps. heatmap_order Row ordering strategy plot_type = "rss_heatmap" plot_type = "activity_heatmap". "cluster" keeps existing dendrogram-based order, "group" groups regulons group regulon reaches maximum heatmap value, "input" keeps resolved feature order. "group" "input" disable row clustering chosen order preserved. heatmap_row_names_side, heatmap_column_names_side Sides used row column names SCENIC heatmaps. heatmap_row_names_rot, heatmap_column_names_rot Rotation angles row column names SCENIC heatmaps. heatmap_border Whether draw heatmap borders SCENIC heatmaps. heatmap_palette, heatmap_palcolor Palette passed GroupHeatmap() FeatureHeatmap() SCENIC heatmaps. heatmap_palette = NULL, sensible default selected heatmap type. heatmap_group_palette, heatmap_group_palcolor Group annotation palette passed GroupHeatmap() FeatureHeatmap() SCENIC heatmaps. heatmap_limits Optional two-length numeric vector used color scale limits plot_type = "rss_heatmap" plot_type = "activity_heatmap". example, c(-2, 2) fixes z-score heatmaps legend range. heatmap_args Additional arguments passed GroupHeatmap() plot_type = "activity_heatmap" FeatureHeatmap() plot_type = "rss_heatmap". max_targets Maximum number target genes shown per TF/regulon network-style plots. max_edges Maximum number TF-target edges shown global network plots. Edges ranked absolute weight weight column present. network_layout Graph layout used network plots. "fr" matches force-directed Fruchterman-Reingold layout used Pando examples. network_tf Optional TF names used plot_type = "network". NULL, features, highlight_tf, top RSS regulons used. label_nodes nodes label network plots. network_label_top_n Maximum number high-degree TF nodes labeled plot_type = "network_graph" label_nodes = "tfs". combine Whether combine group plots patchwork::wrap_plots(). ncol Number columns used combine = TRUE. return_data Whether return RSS matrices ranking tables together plots. FALSE, plot object plot list returned. title Optional title added combined plot. point_color Color regulon rank points. top_color Color top regulon rank points. point_size Point size. point_alpha Alpha regulon rank points. highlight_tf Optional TF regulon names highlight every group plot. Values can match either TF regulon, example "Sox9" "Sox9(+)". highlight_color Color highlighted TF regulon points rank lines. highlight_point_size Point size highlighted TFs regulons. highlight_linewidth Line width highlighted TF regulon rank lines. label_size Text size top regulon labels. label_max_overlaps Maximum number overlapping labels allowed ggrepel::geom_text_repel() dropping label. default Inf keeps requested top highlighted TF labels. verbose Whether print messages. ... Additional arguments passed directly underlying GroupHeatmap() FeatureHeatmap() call plot_type "activity_heatmap" "rss_heatmap". example, width height can supplied directly.

title: Plot top regulon specificity scores from SCENIC results — SCENICPlot
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(pancreas_sub) pancreas_sub <- standard_scop(pancreas_sub) pancreas_sub <- RunSCENIC(   pancreas_sub,   species = "Mus_musculus",   backend = "cpp",   work_dir = "test/scenic" )  scenic_rss <- SCENICPlot(   pancreas_sub,   group.by = "CellType",   plot_type = "rss_rank" ) scenic_rss$plot example_regulons <- unique(scenic_rss$top_table$regulon)[1:2] example_tfs <- unique(scenic_rss$top_table$TF)[1:2]  SCENICPlot(pancreas_sub, group.by = "CellType", plot_type = "rss_heatmap") SCENICPlot(   pancreas_sub,   group.by = "CellType",   plot_type = "rss_heatmap",   width = 2,   height = 3 ) SCENICPlot(pancreas_sub, group.by = "CellType", plot_type = "rss_dotplot") SCENICPlot(pancreas_sub, group.by = "CellType", plot_type = "activity_heatmap") SCENICPlot(   pancreas_sub,   group.by = "CellType",   plot_type = "rss_heatmap",   heatmap_order = "group",   heatmap_cluster_columns = FALSE ) SCENICPlot(   pancreas_sub,   group.by = "CellType",   plot_type = "rss_heatmap",   rss_scale = TRUE,   heatmap_order = "group",   heatmap_limits = c(-2, 2) ) SCENICPlot(   pancreas_sub,   group.by = "CellType",   plot_type = "activity_heatmap",   activity_scale = TRUE,   heatmap_limits = c(-2, 2) ) SCENICPlot(   pancreas_sub,   group.by = "CellType",   plot_type = "activity_violin",   features = example_regulons ) SCENICPlot(   pancreas_sub,   group.by = "CellType",   plot_type = "activity_dim",   features = example_regulons ) SCENICPlot(   pancreas_sub,   group.by = "CellType",   plot_type = "activity_cor_dumbbell",   features = example_regulons,   cor.features = c("nFeature_RNA", "nCount_RNA") ) SCENICPlot(pancreas_sub, group.by = "CellType", plot_type = "regulon_size") SCENICPlot(   pancreas_sub,   group.by = "CellType",   plot_type = "network_graph",   max_targets = 10,   max_edges = 500,   label_nodes = "tfs" ) SCENICPlot(   pancreas_sub,   group.by = "CellType",   plot_type = "network",   network_tf = example_tfs,   max_targets = 30 ) SCENICPlot(   pancreas_sub,   group.by = "CellType",   plot_type = "target_bar",   features = example_regulons,   max_targets = 20 ) } # }

code: Scanorama_integrate(   srt_merge = NULL,   batch = NULL,   append = TRUE,   srt_list = NULL,   assay = NULL,   do_normalization = NULL,   normalization_method = "LogNormalize",   do_HVF_finding = TRUE,   HVF_source = "separate",   HVF_method = "vst",   nHVF = 2000,   HVF_min_intersection = 1,   HVF = NULL,   do_scaling = TRUE,   vars_to_regress = NULL,   regression_model = "linear",   Scanorama_dims_use = NULL,   nonlinear_reduction = "umap",   nonlinear_reduction_dims = c(2, 3),   nonlinear_reduction_params = list(),   force_nonlinear_reduction = TRUE,   neighbor_metric = "euclidean",   neighbor_k = 20L,   cluster_algorithm = "louvain",   cluster_resolution = 0.6,   return_corrected = FALSE,   Scanorama_params = list(),   verbose = TRUE,   seed = 11 )

title: The Scanorama integration function — Scanorama_integrate
text: srt_merge merged `Seurat` object includes batch information. batch character string specifying batch variable name. append Whether integrated data appended original Seurat object (srt_merge). Default TRUE. srt_list list Seurat objects checked preprocessed. assay assay use. NULL, default assay Seurat object used. object also contains ChromatinAssay, default assay additional ChromatinAssay preprocessed sequentially. do_normalization Whether data normalization performed. Default TRUE. normalization_method normalization method used. Possible values "LogNormalize", "SCT", "TFIDF", "scran". Default "LogNormalize". do_HVF_finding Whether perform high variable feature finding. TRUE, function force find highly variable features (HVF) using specified HVF method. HVF_source source highly variable features. Possible values "global" "separate". Default "separate". HVF_method method use finding highly variable features. Options "vst", "mvp", "disp", "scran". Default "vst". nHVF number highly variable features select. NULL, highly variable features used. Default 2000. HVF_min_intersection feature needs present batches minimum number times order considered highly variable. Default 1. HVF vector feature names use highly variable features. NULL, function use highly variable features identified HVF method. do_scaling Whether perform scaling. TRUE, function force scaling package ScaleData path. vars_to_regress vector variable names include additional regression variables. Default NULL. regression_model regression model use scaling. Options "linear", "poisson", "negativebinomial". Default "linear". Scanorama_dims_use vector specifying dimensions returned Scanorama utilized downstream cell cluster finding nonlinear reduction. set NULL, returned dimensions used default. nonlinear_reduction nonlinear dimensionality reduction method use. Options "umap", "umap-naive", "tsne", "dm", "phate", "pacmap", "trimap", "largevis", "fr". Default "umap". nonlinear_reduction_dims number dimensions keep nonlinear dimensionality reduction. vector provided, different numbers dimensions can specified method. Default c(2, 3). nonlinear_reduction_params list parameters pass nonlinear dimensionality reduction method. force_nonlinear_reduction Whether force nonlinear dimensionality reduction even specified reduction already present Seurat object. Default TRUE. neighbor_metric distance metric use finding neighbors. Options "euclidean", "cosine", "manhattan", "hamming". Default "euclidean". neighbor_k number nearest neighbors use finding neighbors. Default 20. cluster_algorithm clustering algorithm use. Options "louvain", "slm", "leiden". Default "louvain". cluster_resolution resolution parameter use clustering. Larger values result fewer clusters. Default 0.6. return_corrected Logical indicating whether return corrected data. Default `FALSE`. Scanorama_params list parameters scanorama.correct function. Default `list()`. verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE. seed Random seed reproducibility. Default 11.

text: srt Seurat object RunScissor. plot_type Plot type. "umap" shows embedding panels, "heatmap" shows FeatureHeatmap, statistical views "bar" "upset" drawn thisplot::StatPlot. reduction dimensionality reduction use. specified, use reduction returned DefaultReduction. prefix Prefix used RunScissor. group.Optional metadata column shown together Scissor status. "heatmap", default Scissor status column. statistical plots, passed thisplot::StatPlot, except "upset" uses split Scissor status distributions group. split.Name column meta.data column split plot . Default NULL. features character vector features use. "variance" ranks genes variance selected cells, "status_diff" ranks largest mean-expression difference Scissor status groups, "coef_cor" ranks absolute correlation Scissor coefficients, "input_order" keeps RunScissor input order. tool_name Name srt@tools entry created RunScissor. status Scissor status levels included heatmap. include.background Whether include background cells heatmap background status upset plots. upset_top_n Maximum number group.levels show plot_type = "upset". frequent levels kept. NULL keeps . cells character vector cell names use. layer layer use. Default "counts". assay assay use. NULL, default assay Seurat object used. object also contains ChromatinAssay, default assay additional ChromatinAssay preprocessed sequentially. max_cells integer, maximum number cells sample per group. Default 100. cell_order vector cell names defining order cells. Default NULL. exp_method character vector specifying method calculating expression values. Options "zscore", "raw", "fc", "log2fc", "log1p". Default "zscore". stat_type Set stat types ("percent" "count"). combine Whether combine UMAP panels StatPlot panels. nrow, ncol, byrow Layout parameters passed patchwork::wrap_plots() thisplot::StatPlot. pt.size size points plot. pt.alpha transparency data points. Default 1. palette Color palette name. Available palettes can found thisplot::show_palettes. Default "Chinese". palcolor Custom colors used create color palette. Default "Chinese". group_palcolor character vector specifying group color use. Default NULL. cell_annotation character vector specifying cell annotation(s) include. Default NULL. show_row_names Whether show row names heatmap. Default FALSE. show_column_names Whether show column names heatmap. Default FALSE. cluster_rows Whether cluster rows heatmap. Default FALSE. cluster_columns Whether cluster columns heatmap. Default FALSE. theme_use, theme_args Theme used thisplot::StatPlot. verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE. ... code: data(panc8_sub) data(islet_bulk) panc8_sub <- RunScissor(   panc8_sub,   bulk_dataset = islet_bulk,   condition.by = "condition",   positive = "bfa",   family = "binomial",   features = head(intersect(     rownames(panc8_sub),     rownames(SummarizedExperiment::assay(islet_bulk, "counts"))   ), 1000),   alpha = 0.2,   cutoff = 0.5 ) panc8_sub <- standard_scop(panc8_sub, verbose = FALSE)  ScissorPlot(   panc8_sub,   xlab = "UMAP_1",   ylab = "UMAP_2" )  ScissorPlot(   panc8_sub,   group.by = "celltype",   xlab = "UMAP_1",   ylab = "UMAP_2" )  ht <- ScissorPlot(   panc8_sub,   plot_type = "heatmap",   group.by = "celltype" ) ht$plot  ScissorPlot(   panc8_sub,   plot_type = "bar",   group.by = "celltype" )  ScissorPlot(   panc8_sub,   plot_type = "upset" )  ScissorPlot(   panc8_sub,   plot_type = "rose",   label = TRUE )  ScissorPlot(   panc8_sub,   plot_type = "ring",   label = TRUE )  ScissorPlot(   panc8_sub,   plot_type = "pie",   label = TRUE )  ScissorPlot(   panc8_sub,   plot_type = "dot",   label = TRUE )

code: SeuratToScopGiotto(   srt,   assay = NULL,   layer = "counts",   sct.assay = "SCT",   use_sct = c("auto", "none", "normalized"),   image = NULL,   coord.cols = c("x", "y"),   features = NULL,   conversion_params = list(),   use_official = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE,   seed = 11 )

title: Convert Seurat to an internal Giotto workflow object — SeuratToScopGiotto
text: srt Seurat object. assay assay use. NULL, default assay Seurat object used. object also contains ChromatinAssay, default assay additional ChromatinAssay preprocessed sequentially. layer Assay layer used expression matrix. sct.assay Name SCT assay. use_sct handle SCT data. `"auto"` keeps counts main Giotto expression records SCT availability. `"none"` ignores SCT. `"normalized"` adds SCT normalized values additional expression layer. image Name Seurat spatial image used spatial workflow. NULL, first image used present. coord.cols Metadata coordinate columns used spatial workflow image available. features Features used PCA clustering. NULL, current variable features used, falling back assay features. conversion_params Additional parameters passed Giotto::createGiottoObject(). use_official Whether try `Giotto::seuratToGiottoV5()` falling back scop-controlled converter. verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE. seed Random seed reproducibility. Default 11.

title: Convert Seurat to an internal Giotto workflow object — SeuratToScopGiotto
text: `giotto2` object.

title: Convert Seurat to an internal Giotto workflow object — SeuratToScopGiotto
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Convert Seurat/SCT data into a standalone Giotto workflow object. g <- SeuratToScopGiotto(   spatial_seurat,   assay = "RNA",   layer = "counts",   use_sct = "normalized" )  # Run the basic Giotto pipeline and plot directly from the Giotto object. g <- RunGiottoWorkflow(g, steps = "basic") GiottoPlot(g, plot_type = "cluster") GiottoPlot(g, plot_type = "network") GiottoPlot(g, plot_type = "dim")  # Add selected Giotto analyses without writing to the Seurat object. g <- GiottoSpatialGenes(g, top_n = 50) g <- GiottoCellProximity(g, group.by = "celltype", number_of_simulations = 100) GiottoPlot(g, plot_type = "spatial_genes", top_n = 20) GiottoPlot(g, plot_type = "cell_proximity")  # Export a result back to Seurat only when explicitly requested. spatial_seurat <- AddGiottoToSeurat(   spatial_seurat,   g,   result = "cluster",   name = "Giotto_cluster" ) } # }

title: Train a CellTypist model — TrainCellTypist
text: Depends return: summary list, model path, Python model object.

title: Train a CellTypist model — TrainCellTypist
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(pancreas_sub) pancreas_sub <- standard_scop(pancreas_sub)  model_info <- TrainCellTypist(   srt = pancreas_sub,   labels = "SubCellType",   model_path = tempfile(fileext = ".pkl") )  data(panc8_sub) genenames <- make.unique(   thisutils::capitalize(     rownames(panc8_sub),     force_tolower = TRUE   ) ) names(genenames) <- rownames(panc8_sub) panc8_sub <- RenameFeatures(   panc8_sub,   newnames = genenames ) panc8_sub <- standard_scop(panc8_sub)  pancreas_sub <- RunCellTypist(   srt = pancreas_sub,   model = model_info ) CellDimPlot(   pancreas_sub,   group.by = c("SubCellType", "celltypist_predicted_labels") )  panc8_sub <- RunCellTypist(   srt = panc8_sub,   model = model_info ) CellDimPlot(   panc8_sub,   group.by = c("celltype", "celltypist_predicted_labels") )  ht <- CellCorHeatmap(   srt_query = panc8_sub,   srt_ref = panc8_sub,   query_group = "celltypist_predicted_labels",   ref_group = "celltype",   width = 4,   height = 3 ) ht$plot } # }
