title: The Uncorrected integration function — Uncorrected_integrate
text: srt_merge merged `Seurat` object includes batch information. batch character string specifying batch variable name. append Whether integrated data appended original Seurat object (srt_merge). Default TRUE. srt_list list Seurat objects checked preprocessed. assay assay use. NULL, default assay Seurat object used. object also contains ChromatinAssay, default assay additional ChromatinAssay preprocessed sequentially. do_normalization Whether data normalization performed. Default TRUE. normalization_method normalization method used. Possible values "LogNormalize", "SCT", "TFIDF", "scran". Default "LogNormalize". do_HVF_finding Whether perform high variable feature finding. TRUE, function force find highly variable features (HVF) using specified HVF method. HVF_source source highly variable features. Possible values "global" "separate". Default "separate". HVF_method method use finding highly variable features. Options "vst", "mvp", "disp", "scran". Default "vst". nHVF number highly variable features select. NULL, highly variable features used. Default 2000. HVF_min_intersection feature needs present batches minimum number times order considered highly variable. Default 1. HVF vector feature names use highly variable features. NULL, function use highly variable features identified HVF method. do_scaling Whether perform scaling. TRUE, function force scaling package ScaleData path. vars_to_regress vector variable names include additional regression variables. Default NULL. regression_model regression model use scaling. Options "linear", "poisson", "negativebinomial". Default "linear". scale_within_batch Whether scale data within batch. valid integration_method one "Uncorrected", "Seurat", "MNN", "Harmony", "BBKNN", "CSS", "ComBat". linear_reduction linear dimensionality reduction method use. Options "pca", "svd", "ica", "nmf", "mds", "glmpca". Default "pca". linear_reduction_dims number dimensions keep linear dimensionality reduction. Default 50. linear_reduction_dims_use dimensions use downstream analysis. NULL, estimated dimensions stored linear reduction used available; otherwise, first 50 dimensions used fallback. linear_reduction_params list parameters pass linear dimensionality reduction method. force_linear_reduction Whether force linear dimensionality reduction even specified reduction already present Seurat object. nonlinear_reduction nonlinear dimensionality reduction method use. Options "umap", "umap-naive", "tsne", "dm", "phate", "pacmap", "trimap", "largevis", "fr". Default "umap". nonlinear_reduction_dims number dimensions keep nonlinear dimensionality reduction. vector provided, different numbers dimensions can specified method. Default c(2, 3). nonlinear_reduction_params list parameters pass nonlinear dimensionality reduction method. force_nonlinear_reduction Whether force nonlinear dimensionality reduction even specified reduction already present Seurat object. Default TRUE. neighbor_metric distance metric use finding neighbors. Options "euclidean", "cosine", "manhattan", "hamming". Default "euclidean". neighbor_k number nearest neighbors use finding neighbors. Default 20. cluster_algorithm clustering algorithm use. Options "louvain", "slm", "leiden". Default "louvain". cluster_resolution resolution parameter use clustering. Larger values result fewer clusters. Default 0.6. verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE. seed Random seed reproducibility. Default 11.

title: Velocity Plot — VelocityPlot
text: function creates velocity plot given Seurat object. plot shows velocity vectors cells specified reduction space.

title: Velocity Plot — VelocityPlot
code: VelocityPlot(   srt,   reduction,   dims = c(1, 2),   cells = NULL,   velocity = "stochastic",   plot_type = c("raw", "grid", "stream"),   group.by = NULL,   group_palette = "Chinese",   group_palcolor = NULL,   n_neighbors = ceiling(ncol(srt@assays[[1]])/50),   density = 1,   smooth = 0.5,   scale = 1,   min_mass = 1,   cutoff_perc = 5,   arrow_angle = 20,   arrow_color = "black",   streamline_L = 5,   streamline_minL = 1,   streamline_res = 1,   streamline_n = 15,   streamline_width = c(0, 0.8),   streamline_alpha = 1,   streamline_color = NULL,   streamline_palette = "RdYlBu",   streamline_palcolor = NULL,   streamline_bg_color = "white",   streamline_bg_stroke = 0.5,   aspect.ratio = 1,   title = "Cell velocity",   subtitle = NULL,   xlab = NULL,   ylab = NULL,   legend.position = "right",   legend.direction = "vertical",   theme_use = "theme_scop",   theme_args = list(),   return_layer = FALSE,   seed = 11,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Velocity Plot — VelocityPlot
text: srt Seurat object. reduction dimensionality reduction use. specified, use reduction returned DefaultReduction. dims Dimensions plot, must two-length numeric vector specifying x- y-dimensions cells character vector cell names use. velocity Name velocity use plotting. Default "stochastic". plot_type Type plot create. Can "raw", "grid", "stream". group.Name one meta.data columns group (color) cells . group_palette Name palette use coloring groups. Defaults "Chinese". group_palcolor Colors use coloring groups. Defaults NULL. n_neighbors Number neighbors include density estimation. Defaults ceiling(ncol(srt@assays[[1]]) / 50). density Proportion cells plot. Defaults 1 (plot cells). smooth Smoothing parameter density estimation. Defaults 0.5. scale Scaling factor velocity vectors. Defaults 1. min_mass Minimum mass value density-based cutoff. Defaults 1. cutoff_perc Percentile value density-based cutoff. Defaults 5. arrow_angle Angle arrowheads. Defaults 20. arrow_color Color arrowheads. Defaults "black". streamline_L Length streamlines. Defaults 5. streamline_minL Minimum length streamlines. Defaults 1. streamline_res Resolution streamlines. Defaults 1. streamline_n Number streamlines plot. Defaults 15. streamline_width Width streamlines. Defaults c(0, 0.8). streamline_alpha Alpha transparency streamlines. Defaults 1. streamline_color Color streamlines. Defaults NULL. streamline_palette Name palette use coloring streamlines. Defaults "RdYlBu". streamline_palcolor Colors use coloring streamlines. Defaults NULL. streamline_bg_color Background color streamlines. Defaults "white". streamline_bg_stroke Stroke width streamlines background. Defaults 0.5. aspect.ratio Aspect ratio panel. Default 1. title text title. Defaults "Cell velocity". subtitle text subtitle plot displayed title. Default NULL. xlab x-axis label plot. Default NULL. ylab y-axis label plot. Default NULL. legend.position position legends, one "none", "left", "right", "bottom", "top". Default "right". legend.direction direction legend plot. Can one "vertical" "horizontal". theme_use Theme used. Can character string theme function. Default "theme_scop". theme_args arguments passed theme_use. Default list(). return_layer Whether return plot layers list. Defaults FALSE. seed Random seed reproducibility. Default 11. verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE.

title: Velocity Plot — VelocityPlot
code: data(pancreas_sub) pancreas_sub <- standard_scop(pancreas_sub) pancreas_sub <- RunSCVELO(   pancreas_sub,   group.by = "SubCellType",   linear_reduction = "pca",   nonlinear_reduction = "umap",   backend = "cpp",   return_seurat = TRUE ) VelocityPlot(   pancreas_sub,   reduction = "umap" )  VelocityPlot(   pancreas_sub,   reduction = "umap",   group.by = "SubCellType" )  VelocityPlot(   pancreas_sub,   reduction = "umap",   plot_type = "grid" )  VelocityPlot(   pancreas_sub,   reduction = "umap",   plot_type = "stream" )  VelocityPlot(   pancreas_sub,   reduction = "umap",   plot_type = "stream",   streamline_color = "black" )  VelocityPlot(   pancreas_sub,   reduction = "umap",   plot_type = "stream",   streamline_color = "black",   arrow_color = "red" )

title: Volcano Plot — VolcanoPlot
text: Generate volcano plot based differential expression analysis results.

title: Volcano Plot — VolcanoPlot
code: VolcanoPlot(   srt,   group.by = NULL,   test.use = "wilcox",   res = NULL,   group_use = NULL,   DE_threshold = "avg_log2FC > 0 & p_val_adj < 0.05",   x_metric = NULL,   y_metric = NULL,   palette = "RdBu",   palcolor = NULL,   pt.size = 1,   pt.alpha = 1,   cols.background = "grey80",   cols.highlight = "black",   sizes.highlight = 1,   alpha.highlight = 1,   stroke.highlight = 0.5,   nlabel = 5,   features_label = NULL,   only.pos = FALSE,   label.by = c("p_val_adj", "p_val", "diff_pct", "avg_log2FC"),   label.fg = "black",   label.bg = "white",   label.bg.r = 0.1,   label.size = 4,   aspect.ratio = NULL,   xlab = NULL,   ylab = NULL,   theme_use = "theme_scop",   theme_args = list(),   combine = TRUE,   nrow = NULL,   ncol = NULL,   byrow = TRUE,   threshold_method = c("rectangular", "hyperbolic"),   hyperbola_c = 6,   annotate_enrichment = FALSE,   enrich_from = c("Enrichment", "GSEA", "GSVA"),   enrich_db = NULL,   enrich_terms = NULL,   enrich_top_terms = 3,   enrich_padj_cutoff = 0.05,   enrich_gsva_score_cutoff = NULL,   gsva_method = NULL,   enrich_nlabel = 15,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Volcano Plot — VolcanoPlot
text: srt Seurat object SummarizedExperiment object containing results differential expression analysis. group.Name one meta.data columns group (color) cells . test.use character string specifying type statistical test use. Default "wilcox". res data.frame data.table differential expression results. res provided, srt ignored. data.frame must contain columns: gene, group1 (factor character), avg_log2FC, p_val_adj, optionally pct.1 pct.2 calculating diff_pct. group_use Groups plot. Default NULL (groups). DE_threshold character string specifying threshold differential expression (used highlight significant genes plot types). Default "p_val < 0.05" sample-level methods ("edgeR" "limma") "avg_log2FC > 0 & p_val_adj < 0.05" otherwise. x_metric character string specifying metric use x-axis (volcano plot). Default NULL, uses "avg_log2FC" sample-level methods ("edgeR" "limma") "diff_pct" otherwise. y_metric character string specifying metric use y-axis. Options: "p_val" "p_val_adj". Default "p_val" sample-level methods ("edgeR" "limma") "p_val_adj" otherwise. palette Color palette name. Available palettes can found thisplot::show_palettes. Default "RdBu". palcolor Custom colors used create color palette. Default NULL. pt.size size points. Default 1. pt.alpha transparency data points. Default 1. cols.background character string specifying color non-DE background points volcano plots. Default "grey80". cols.highlight character string specifying color highlighted points. Default "black". sizes.highlight size highlighted points. Default 1. alpha.highlight transparency highlighted points. Default 1. stroke.highlight stroke width highlighted points. Default 0.5. nlabel integer value specifying number labeled points per group. Default 5. features_label character vector specifying feature labels plot. Default NULL. .pos Whether show positive log2 fold-change results differential expression visualizations. Default FALSE. label.Metric used select automatic labels features_label = NULL. Options "p_val_adj", "p_val", "diff_pct", "avg_log2FC". Smaller p-values ranked first; diff_pct avg_log2FC use strongest positive negative effects within group. Default "p_val_adj". label.fg character string specifying color labels' foreground. Default "black". label.bg character string specifying color labels' background. Default "white". label.bg.r radius rounding labels' background. Default 0.1. label.size size labels. Default 4. aspect.ratio Aspect ratio panel. Default NULL. xlab character string specifying x-axis label. ylab character string specifying y-axis label. theme_use Theme use plot. Default "theme_scop". theme_args list additional arguments pass theme function. Default list(). combine Whether combine multiple plots one. Default TRUE. nrow Number rows combined plots. Default NULL. ncol Number columns combined plots. Default NULL. byrow Whether fill plots row. Default TRUE. threshold_method Volcano significance threshold method. Options "rectangular" (legacy DE_threshold) "hyperbolic" (|log2FC * -log10(padj)| > c). Default "rectangular". hyperbola_c Numeric cutoff c hyperbolic volcano threshold. Default 6. annotate_enrichment Whether annotate enrichment-hit genes volcano plots. Enrichment results read existing results srt@tools . Default FALSE. enrich_from Character vector specifying enrichment result source(s) annotate. Options "Enrichment", "GSEA", "GSVA". Default c("Enrichment", "GSEA", "GSVA"). enrich_db Optional database filter enrichment annotation, e.g. "GO_BP" "KEGG". Default NULL. enrich_terms Optional whitelist enrichment term IDs names annotation. Default NULL. enrich_top_terms Number top enriched terms selected per source/group/database. Default 3. enrich_padj_cutoff Adjusted p-value cutoff "Enrichment" "GSEA" annotation. Default 0.05. enrich_gsva_score_cutoff Optional absolute GSVA score cutoff "GSVA" annotation. Default NULL. gsva_method Optional GSVA method filter (e.g. "gsva" "ssgsea") multiple GSVA tool slots exist. Default NULL. enrich_nlabel Maximum number enrichment-derived labels added per group. Labels features_label always retained. Default 15. verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE.

title: Volcano Plot — VolcanoPlot
code: data(pancreas_sub) pancreas_sub <- standard_scop(pancreas_sub) pancreas_sub <- RunDEtest(   pancreas_sub,   group.by = "CellType" ) VolcanoPlot(   pancreas_sub,   group.by = "CellType",   ncol = 2 )  VolcanoPlot(   pancreas_sub,   group.by = "CellType",   group_use = c("Ductal", "Endocrine"),   ncol = 2 )  VolcanoPlot(   pancreas_sub,   group.by = "CellType",   DE_threshold = "abs(diff_pct) > 0.3 & p_val_adj < 0.05",   ncol = 2 )  VolcanoPlot(   pancreas_sub,   group.by = "CellType",   x_metric = "avg_log2FC",   y_metric = "p_val",   DE_threshold = "abs(avg_log2FC) > log2(1.5) & p_val < 0.05",   ncol = 2 )  VolcanoPlot(   pancreas_sub,   group.by = "CellType",   threshold_method = "hyperbolic",   hyperbola_c = 6,   ncol = 2 )  pancreas_sub <- RunEnrichment(   pancreas_sub,   group.by = "CellType",   db = "GO_BP",   species = "Mus_musculus" ) VolcanoPlot(   pancreas_sub,   group.by = "CellType",   threshold_method = "hyperbolic",   hyperbola_c = 6,   annotate_enrichment = TRUE,   enrich_from = "Enrichment",   enrich_db = "GO_BP",   ncol = 2 )

code: WNN_integrate(   srt_merge = NULL,   batch = NULL,   append = TRUE,   srt_list = NULL,   assay = NULL,   do_normalization = NULL,   normalization_method = "LogNormalize",   do_HVF_finding = TRUE,   HVF_source = "separate",   HVF_method = "vst",   nHVF = 2000,   HVF_min_intersection = 1,   HVF = NULL,   do_scaling = TRUE,   vars_to_regress = NULL,   regression_model = "linear",   scale_within_batch = FALSE,   linear_reduction = "pca",   linear_reduction_dims = 50,   linear_reduction_dims_use = NULL,   linear_reduction_params = list(),   force_linear_reduction = FALSE,   nonlinear_reduction = "umap",   nonlinear_reduction_dims = c(2, 3),   nonlinear_reduction_params = list(),   force_nonlinear_reduction = TRUE,   neighbor_metric = "euclidean",   neighbor_k = 20L,   cluster_algorithm = "louvain",   cluster_resolution = 0.6,   verbose = TRUE,   seed = 11 )

title: The WNN integration function — WNN_integrate
text: srt_merge merged `Seurat` object includes batch information. batch character string specifying batch variable name. append Whether integrated data appended original Seurat object (srt_merge). Default TRUE. srt_list list Seurat objects checked preprocessed. assay assay use. NULL, default assay Seurat object used. object also contains ChromatinAssay, default assay additional ChromatinAssay preprocessed sequentially. do_normalization Whether data normalization performed. Default TRUE. normalization_method normalization method used. Possible values "LogNormalize", "SCT", "TFIDF", "scran". Default "LogNormalize". do_HVF_finding Whether perform high variable feature finding. TRUE, function force find highly variable features (HVF) using specified HVF method. HVF_source source highly variable features. Possible values "global" "separate". Default "separate". HVF_method method use finding highly variable features. Options "vst", "mvp", "disp", "scran". Default "vst". nHVF number highly variable features select. NULL, highly variable features used. Default 2000. HVF_min_intersection feature needs present batches minimum number times order considered highly variable. Default 1. HVF vector feature names use highly variable features. NULL, function use highly variable features identified HVF method. do_scaling Whether perform scaling. TRUE, function force scaling package ScaleData path. vars_to_regress vector variable names include additional regression variables. Default NULL. regression_model regression model use scaling. Options "linear", "poisson", "negativebinomial". Default "linear". scale_within_batch Whether scale data within batch. valid integration_method one "Uncorrected", "Seurat", "MNN", "Harmony", "BBKNN", "CSS", "ComBat". linear_reduction linear dimensionality reduction method use. Options "pca", "svd", "ica", "nmf", "mds", "glmpca". Default "pca". linear_reduction_dims number dimensions keep linear dimensionality reduction. Default 50. linear_reduction_dims_use dimensions use downstream analysis. NULL, estimated dimensions stored linear reduction used available; otherwise, first 50 dimensions used fallback. linear_reduction_params list parameters pass linear dimensionality reduction method. force_linear_reduction Whether force linear dimensionality reduction even specified reduction already present Seurat object. nonlinear_reduction nonlinear dimensionality reduction method use. Options "umap", "umap-naive", "tsne", "dm", "phate", "pacmap", "trimap", "largevis", "fr". Default "umap". nonlinear_reduction_dims number dimensions keep nonlinear dimensionality reduction. vector provided, different numbers dimensions can specified method. Default c(2, 3). nonlinear_reduction_params list parameters pass nonlinear dimensionality reduction method. force_nonlinear_reduction Whether force nonlinear dimensionality reduction even specified reduction already present Seurat object. Default TRUE. neighbor_metric distance metric use finding neighbors. Options "euclidean", "cosine", "manhattan", "hamming". Default "euclidean". neighbor_k number nearest neighbors use finding neighbors. Default 20. cluster_algorithm clustering algorithm use. Options "louvain", "slm", "leiden". Default "louvain". cluster_resolution resolution parameter use clustering. Larger values result fewer clusters. Default 0.6. verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE. seed Random seed reproducibility. Default 11.

title: The WNN integration function — WNN_integrate
code: data("pbmcmultiome_sub", package = "scop") pbmcmultiome_sub$batch <- rep(c("batch1", "batch2"), length.out = ncol(pbmcmultiome_sub)) pbmcmultiome_sub <- WNN_integrate(   srt_merge = pbmcmultiome_sub,   batch = "batch",   linear_reduction_dims = 20,   linear_reduction_dims_use = 1:10 )

"micromamba" requested micromamba available PATH, package-managed micromamba downloaded automatically. envname name conda-compatible Python environment. NULL, environment name set "scop_env". Default NULL. verbose Whether print environment information.

title: Python environment requirements — env_requirements
text: version Python version environment. Default "3.10-1". include_optional Whether include optional Python dependencies. modules Optional requirement modules include. Supported values "scanpy", "scvi", "scanorama", "bbknn", "celltypist", "cellphonedb", "magic", "scrublet", "doubletdetection", "sccoda", "doublet", "palantir", "scvelo", "cellrank", "wot", "phate", "pacmap", "trimap", "multimap", "scomm", "scenic", "seacells", "tage". NULL, default environment returned. default excludes "sccoda", "scomm", "scenic" workflows require dependency stacks prepared explicitly. "scenic" module standalone always uses Python "3.10-1". verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE.

title: Seurat v5 fastMNN integration — fastMNN5_integrate
text: srt_merge merged `Seurat` object includes batch information. batch character string specifying batch variable name. append Whether integrated data appended original Seurat object (srt_merge). Default TRUE. srt_list list Seurat objects checked preprocessed. assay assay use. NULL, default assay Seurat object used. object also contains ChromatinAssay, default assay additional ChromatinAssay preprocessed sequentially. do_normalization Whether data normalization performed. Default TRUE. normalization_method normalization method used. Possible values "LogNormalize", "SCT", "TFIDF", "scran". Default "LogNormalize". do_HVF_finding Whether perform high variable feature finding. TRUE, function force find highly variable features (HVF) using specified HVF method. HVF_source source highly variable features. Possible values "global" "separate". Default "separate". HVF_method method use finding highly variable features. Options "vst", "mvp", "disp", "scran". Default "vst". nHVF number highly variable features select. NULL, highly variable features used. Default 2000. HVF_min_intersection feature needs present batches minimum number times order considered highly variable. Default 1. HVF vector feature names use highly variable features. NULL, function use highly variable features identified HVF method. fastMNN_dims_use vector specifying integrated dimensions used downstream clustering nonlinear reduction. nonlinear_reduction nonlinear dimensionality reduction method use. Options "umap", "umap-naive", "tsne", "dm", "phate", "pacmap", "trimap", "largevis", "fr". Default "umap". nonlinear_reduction_dims number dimensions keep nonlinear dimensionality reduction. vector provided, different numbers dimensions can specified method. Default c(2, 3). nonlinear_reduction_params list parameters pass nonlinear dimensionality reduction method. force_nonlinear_reduction Whether force nonlinear dimensionality reduction even specified reduction already present Seurat object. Default TRUE. neighbor_metric distance metric use finding neighbors. Options "euclidean", "cosine", "manhattan", "hamming". Default "euclidean". neighbor_k number nearest neighbors use finding neighbors. Default 20. cluster_algorithm clustering algorithm use. Options "louvain", "slm", "leiden". Default "louvain". cluster_resolution resolution parameter use clustering. Larger values result fewer clusters. Default 0.6. IntegrateLayers_params list parameters passed [Seurat::IntegrateLayers]. verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE. seed Random seed reproducibility. Default 11.

title: The integration workflow — integration_scop
text: Integrate single-cell RNA-seq data using various integration methods. ChromatinAssay, current workflow uses TFIDF + SVD/LSI preprocessing. setting, Uncorrected supported directly Harmony5 automatically redirected legacy Harmony workflow. Seurat RPCA currently supported ChromatinAssay.

title: The integration workflow — integration_scop
code: integration_scop(   srt_merge = NULL,   batch,   append = TRUE,   srt_list = NULL,   assay = NULL,   integration_method = c("Uncorrected", "Seurat", "CCA", "RPCA", "scVI", "PeakVI",     "PoissonVI", "WNN", "MultiMAP", "GLUE", "scVI5", "MNN", "fastMNN", "fastMNN5",     "Harmony", "Harmony5", "Scanorama", "BBKNN", "CSS", "Coralysis", "LIGER", "Conos",     "ComBat"),   compute_lisi = FALSE,   lisi_label_colnames = NULL,   lisi_reduction = NULL,   lisi_dims = NULL,   lisi_prefix = NULL,   lisi_tool_name = NULL,   lisi_perplexity = 30,   lisi_tol = 1e-05,   lisi_max_iter = 50,   compute_metrics = FALSE,   metrics_batch_col = NULL,   metrics_celltype_col = NULL,   metrics_reduction = NULL,   metrics_cluster_col = NULL,   metrics_tool_name = NULL,   metrics_k_graph = 15,   do_normalization = NULL,   normalization_method = "LogNormalize",   do_HVF_finding = TRUE,   HVF_source = "separate",   HVF_method = "vst",   nHVF = 2000,   HVF_min_intersection = 1,   HVF = NULL,   do_scaling = TRUE,   vars_to_regress = NULL,   regression_model = "linear",   scale_within_batch = FALSE,   linear_reduction = "pca",   linear_reduction_dims = 50,   linear_reduction_dims_use = NULL,   linear_reduction_params = list(),   force_linear_reduction = FALSE,   nonlinear_reduction = "umap",   nonlinear_reduction_dims = c(2, 3),   nonlinear_reduction_params = list(),   force_nonlinear_reduction = TRUE,   neighbor_metric = "euclidean",   neighbor_k = 20L,   cluster_algorithm = "louvain",   cluster_resolution = 0.6,   seed = 11,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )

title: The integration workflow — integration_scop
text: srt_merge merged `Seurat` object includes batch information. batch character string specifying batch variable name. append Whether integrated data appended original Seurat object (srt_merge). Default TRUE. srt_list list Seurat objects checked preprocessed. assay assay use. NULL, default assay Seurat object used. object also contains ChromatinAssay, default assay additional ChromatinAssay preprocessed sequentially. integration_method character vector specifying integration method use. Supported methods : "Uncorrected", "Seurat", "CCA", "RPCA", "scVI", "PeakVI", "PoissonVI", "WNN", "MultiMAP", "GLUE", "scVI5", "MNN", "fastMNN", "fastMNN5", "Harmony", "Harmony5", "Scanorama", "BBKNN", "CSS", "Coralysis", "LIGER", "Conos", "ComBat". Default "Uncorrected". ChromatinAssay, prefer "Uncorrected" "Harmony5"; latter automatically switched "Harmony". compute_lisi Whether compute LISI scores integrated result. Default FALSE. lisi_label_colnames Character vector metadata columns used compute LISI. NULL compute_lisi = TRUE, batch used single metadata column name. lisi_reduction Dimensional reduction used LISI computation. Default NULL, uses DefaultReduction() integrated object. lisi_dims Dimensions used lisi_reduction. Default NULL, uses available dimensions. lisi_prefix Prefix used storing LISI metadata columns. Default NULL, uses lisi_reduction. lisi_tool_name Name tool entry used store LISI results. Default NULL, uses paste0(lisi_prefix, "_LISI"). lisi_perplexity Effective neighborhood size used LISI. Default 30. lisi_tol Tolerance used LISI binary search. Default 1e-5. lisi_max_iter Maximum iterations used LISI binary search. Default 50. compute_metrics Whether compute integration summary metrics selected reduction. Default FALSE. metrics_batch_col Metadata column used batch-mixing metrics. Default NULL, uses batch single metadata column name. metrics_celltype_col Metadata column used biological conservation metrics. Default NULL. metrics_reduction Reduction used integration metric computation. Default NULL, uses DefaultReduction() integrated object. metrics_cluster_col Metadata column used cluster labels celltype_NMI, celltype_ARI, celltype_purity. Default NULL, resolves integrated cluster column automatically possible. metrics_tool_name Name tool entry used store integration metrics. Default NULL, uses paste0(integration_method, "_metrics"). metrics_k_graph Number neighbors used graph-connectivity computation. Default 15. do_normalization Whether data normalization performed. Default TRUE. normalization_method normalization method used. Possible values "LogNormalize", "SCT", "TFIDF", "scran". Default "LogNormalize". do_HVF_finding Whether perform high variable feature finding. TRUE, function force find highly variable features (HVF) using specified HVF method. HVF_source source highly variable features. Possible values "global" "separate". Default "separate". HVF_method method use finding highly variable features. Options "vst", "mvp", "disp", "scran". Default "vst". nHVF number highly variable features select. NULL, highly variable features used. Default 2000. HVF_min_intersection feature needs present batches minimum number times order considered highly variable. Default 1. HVF vector feature names use highly variable features. NULL, function use highly variable features identified HVF method. do_scaling Whether perform scaling. TRUE, function force scaling package ScaleData path. vars_to_regress vector variable names include additional regression variables. Default NULL. regression_model regression model use scaling. Options "linear", "poisson", "negativebinomial". Default "linear". scale_within_batch Whether scale data within batch. valid integration_method one "Uncorrected", "Seurat", "MNN", "Harmony", "BBKNN", "CSS", "ComBat". linear_reduction linear dimensionality reduction method use. Options "pca", "svd", "ica", "nmf", "mds", "glmpca". Default "pca". linear_reduction_dims number dimensions keep linear dimensionality reduction. Default 50. linear_reduction_dims_use dimensions use downstream analysis. NULL, estimated dimensions stored linear reduction used available; otherwise, first 50 dimensions used fallback. linear_reduction_params list parameters pass linear dimensionality reduction method. force_linear_reduction Whether force linear dimensionality reduction even specified reduction already present Seurat object. nonlinear_reduction nonlinear dimensionality reduction method use. Options "umap", "umap-naive", "tsne", "dm", "phate", "pacmap", "trimap", "largevis", "fr". Default "umap". nonlinear_reduction_dims number dimensions keep nonlinear dimensionality reduction. vector provided, different numbers dimensions can specified method. Default c(2, 3). nonlinear_reduction_params list parameters pass nonlinear dimensionality reduction method. force_nonlinear_reduction Whether force nonlinear dimensionality reduction even specified reduction already present Seurat object. Default TRUE. neighbor_metric distance metric use finding neighbors. Options "euclidean", "cosine", "manhattan", "hamming". Default "euclidean". neighbor_k number nearest neighbors use finding neighbors. Default 20. cluster_algorithm clustering algorithm use. Options "louvain", "slm", "leiden". Default "louvain". cluster_resolution resolution parameter use clustering. Larger values result fewer clusters. Default 0.6. seed Random seed reproducibility. Default 11. verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE. ... Additional arguments passed integration method functions.

title: The integration workflow — integration_scop
text: Seurat object. ChromatinAssay, integrated outputs additionally normalized ATAC naming convention used scop, including *lsi, *UMAP2D, cluster aliases, ATAC_default_* metadata available.

title: The integration workflow — integration_scop
code: data(panc8_sub) panc8_sub <- integration_scop(   panc8_sub,   batch = "tech",   integration_method = "Harmony",   nHVF = 500,   linear_reduction_dims = 20,   linear_reduction_dims_use = 1:10,   nonlinear_reduction_dims = 2,   compute_lisi = TRUE,   lisi_label_colnames = "tech",   lisi_perplexity = 10 ) CellDimPlot(   panc8_sub,   group.by = c("tech", "celltype"),   reduction = "HarmonyUMAP2D" )  LISIPlot(   panc8_sub,   features = c("HarmonyUMAP2D_tech_LISI") )  panc8_sub <- integration_scop(   panc8_sub,   batch = "tech",   integration_method = "LIGER" ) panc8_sub <- integration_scop(   panc8_sub,   batch = "tech",   integration_method = "Harmony",   compute_lisi = TRUE,   lisi_label_colnames = "tech" ) LISIPlot(   panc8_sub,   features = c("UncorrectedpcaUMAP2D_tech_LISI", "HarmonyUMAP2D_tech_LISI") )  data("pbmcmultiome_sub", package = "scop") pbmcmultiome_sub$batch <- rep(c("batch1", "batch2"), length.out = ncol(pbmcmultiome_sub)) pbmcmultiome_sub <- integration_scop(   pbmcmultiome_sub,   batch = "batch",   assay = "peaks",   integration_method = "Harmony5",   normalization_method = "TFIDF" )  integration_methods <- c(   "Uncorrected", "Seurat", "CCA", "RPCA", "scVI", "scVI5",   "MNN", "fastMNN", "fastMNN5", "Harmony", "Harmony5",   "Scanorama", "BBKNN", "CSS", "Coralysis", "LIGER", "Conos", "ComBat" ) p_list <- list() for (method in integration_methods) {   panc8_sub <- integration_scop(     panc8_sub,     batch = "tech",     integration_method = method,     linear_reduction_dims_use = 1:50,     nonlinear_reduction = "umap"   )   p_list[[method]] <- CellDimPlot(     panc8_sub,     group.by = c("tech", "celltype"),     reduction = paste0(method, "UMAP2D"),     xlab = "", ylab = "",     title = method,     legend.position = "none",     theme_use = "theme_blank"   ) }  nonlinear_reductions <- c(   "umap", "tsne", "dm", "phate",   "pacmap", "trimap", "largevis", "fr" ) panc8_sub <- integration_scop(   panc8_sub,   batch = "tech",   integration_method = "Seurat",   linear_reduction_dims_use = 1:50,   nonlinear_reduction = nonlinear_reductions ) for (nr in nonlinear_reductions) {   print(     CellDimPlot(       panc8_sub,       group.by = c("tech", "celltype"),       reduction = paste0("Seurat", nr, "2D"),       xlab = "", ylab = "", title = nr,       legend.position = "none", theme_use = "theme_blank"     )   ) }

what: A subsetted version of human 'panc8' datasets — panc8_sub
title: A subsetted version of human 'panc8' datasets — panc8_sub
text: Human pancreatic islet cell datasets produced across four technologies, SMART-Seq2 (E-MTAB-5061), CelSeq (GSE81076), CelSeq2 (GSE85241), Fluidigm C1 (GSE86469), SeuratData package. data set panc8, 200 cells downsampled form panc8_sub dataset.

title: A subsetted version of human 'panc8' datasets — panc8_sub
text: Seurat object.

title: A subsetted version of human 'panc8' datasets — panc8_sub
A subsetted version of human 'panc8' datasets — panc8_sub
code: if (interactive()) {   data(pancreas_sub)   check_r("satijalab/seurat-data")   library(Seurat)   InstallData <- get_namespace_fun("SeuratData", "InstallData")   InstallData("panc8")   data(panc8)   panc8 <- UpdateSeuratObject(panc8)   set.seed(98)   cells_sub <- unlist(     lapply(       split(colnames(panc8), panc8$dataset),       function(x) sample(x, size = 200)     )   )   panc8_sub <- subset(panc8, cells = cells_sub)   counts <- GetAssayData5(     panc8_sub,     layer = "counts"   )   panc8_sub <- CreateSeuratObject(     counts = counts,     meta.data = panc8_sub@meta.data   )   panc8_sub <- panc8_sub[Matrix::rowSums(counts) > 0, ]   panc8_sub <- panc8_sub[toupper(     rownames(panc8_sub)   ) %in% toupper(     rownames(pancreas_sub)   ), ]   panc8_sub$celltype <- gsub("_", "-", panc8_sub$celltype)   panc8_sub$celltype <- gsub(" ", "-", panc8_sub$celltype)   use_data <- get_namespace_fun("usethis", "use_data")   use_data(     panc8_sub,     compress = "xz",     overwrite = TRUE   ) }

title: A subsetted version of mouse 'pancreas' datasets — pancreas_sub
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ if (interactive()) {   library(Seurat)   library(reticulate)   PrepareEnv()   check_python("scvelo")   scv <- import("scvelo")   adata <- scv$datasets$pancreas()   pancreas <- adata_to_srt(adata)   set.seed(98)   cells <- sample(colnames(pancreas), size = 1000)   pancreas_sub <- pancreas[, cells]   pancreas_sub <- pancreas_sub[Matrix::rowSums(     GetAssayData5(       pancreas_sub,       layer = "counts"     )   ) > 0, ]   pancreas_sub[["CellType"]] <- pancreas_sub[["clusters_coarse"]]   pancreas_sub[["SubCellType"]] <- pancreas_sub[["clusters"]]   pancreas_sub[["clusters_coarse"]] <- pancreas_sub[["clusters"]] <- NULL   pancreas_sub[["Phase"]] <- ifelse(     pancreas_sub$S_score > pancreas_sub$G2M_score,     "S",     "G2M"   )   pancreas_sub[["Phase"]][apply(     pancreas_sub[[]][, c("S_score", "G2M_score")],     1,     max   ) < 0, ] <- "G1"   pancreas_sub[["Phase", drop = TRUE]] <- factor(     pancreas_sub[["Phase", drop = TRUE]],     levels = c("G1", "S", "G2M")   )   pancreas_sub$CellType <- gsub("_", "-", pancreas_sub$CellType)   pancreas_sub$CellType <- gsub(" ", "-", pancreas_sub$CellType)   pancreas_sub$SubCellType <- gsub("_", "-", pancreas_sub$SubCellType)   pancreas_sub$SubCellType <- gsub(" ", "-", pancreas_sub$SubCellType)   pancreas_sub@reductions$X_pca <- NULL   pancreas_sub@reductions$X_umap <- NULL   use_data <- get_namespace_fun("usethis", "use_data")   use_data(     pancreas_sub,     compress = "xz",     overwrite = TRUE   ) } } # }

title: Remove Python packages from a conda-compatible Python environment — remove_python
text: packages character vector package names remove. envname name conda-compatible Python environment. NULL, environment name set "scop_env". Default NULL. conda path command name conda-compatible executable (conda, mamba, micromamba). Use "auto" allow automatically finding appropriate environment manager. "micromamba" requested micromamba available PATH, package-managed micromamba downloaded automatically. pip Whether use pip package removal. Default FALSE (use conda). force Whether force removal without confirmation. Default FALSE. verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE.

title: Plot scFEA module flux heatmap — scFEAHeatmap
text: srt Seurat object returned [RunscFEA()]. assay Flux assay name. layer Flux assay layer. group.Metadata column used aggregate cells. features Optional scFEA modules plot. Supports Seurat-safe feature ids (`"M-1"`), scFEA ids (`"M_1"`), labels (`"M1"`), reaction labels. modules Optional scFEA modules plot. user-facing alias `features` also accepts combined labels `"M150: PRPP -> UMP"`. label_by Row label mode: module id, reaction, . add_sm_anno Whether add `SM_anno` row annotation split rows pathway class. scale_rows Whether z-score row group aggregation. cluster_rows, cluster_columns Passed [ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap()]. sm_anno_label_rot Rotation angle `SM_anno` row-split labels. show_row_names Whether show row labels. show_column_names Whether show aggregated group labels. heatmap_limit Numeric clipping limit row z-scores. heatmap_column_width Width aggregated group column. Numeric values interpreted millimeters. column_names_rot Rotation angle heatmap column labels. heatmap_palette, heatmap_palcolor Continuous heatmap palette passed `palette_colors()`. group_palette, group_palcolor Palette top group annotation. feature_split_palette, feature_split_palcolor Palette `SM_anno` row annotation. border Whether draw borders around heatmap cells annotations. use_raster, raster_by_magick Raster settings passed [ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap()]. column_names_gp, column_title_gp, row_title_gp Font settings passed [grid::gpar()]-aware ComplexHeatmap arguments. legend_title_gp, legend_labels_gp Font settings heatmap annotation legends. row_gap Gap `SM_anno` row splits. sm_anno_size Size left `SM_anno` annotation strip. group_anno_size Size top group annotation strip. mark_features Optional scFEA modules mark [ComplexHeatmap::anno_mark()]. Accepts module ids labels `features`. mark_label_by Label mode marked rows. Defaults `label_by`. mark_labels_gp Font settings marked row labels. `NULL`, compact default chosen `mark_label_by`. mark_annotation_width Width right mark annotation. Numeric values interpreted millimeters. mark_link_width Link width used [ComplexHeatmap::anno_mark()]. name Heatmap legend title. ht_params Additional parameters passed [ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap()], overriding defaults names overlap. width, height Suggested export size. Stored attributes returned heatmap object. verbose Whether print messages.

code: scTenifoldKnkPlot(   srt,   tool_name = "scTenifoldKnk",   plot_type = c("qq", "effect", "network", "manifold"),   top_n = 20,   FDR_threshold = 0.05,   FC_threshold = 1,   features = NULL,   label = TRUE,   nlabel = 10,   pt.size = 2,   pt.alpha = 0.85,   label.size = 3.5,   edge_top_n = 100,   edge_threshold = NULL,   arrow.length = 0.14,   arrow.linewidth = 0.8,   arrow.alpha = 0.8,   start.pt.size = 2.2,   end.pt.size = 3,   manifold.aspect.ratio = 1,   cols.sig = "#D7301F",   cols.ns = "grey70",   cols.ko = "#542788",   xlab = NULL,   ylab = NULL,   title = NULL,   theme_use = "theme_scop",   theme_args = list(),   verbose = TRUE )

title: scTenifoldKnk Plot — scTenifoldKnkPlot
text: srt Seurat object containing scTenifoldKnk result srt@tools. tool_name Name srt@tools entry created RunscTenifoldKnk. plot_type Plot type. "qq" shows common scTenifoldKnk FC versus theoretical chi-square quantile plot. "effect" ranks top perturbed genes FC. "network" draws knockout-centered WT network subgraph saved tensor networks using thisplot::GraphPlot. "manifold" draws paired WT--KO displacement arrows selected genes non-linear manifold alignment space, showing gene's network context shifts virtual knockout. top_n Number top genes show label. FDR_threshold Adjusted p-value cutoff used mark significant genes. FC_threshold FC cutoff used mark significant genes. features Character vector genes show label. plot_type = "network" "manifold", genes included displayed subset present. label Whether label genes. nlabel Number labels add features = NULL. pt.size Point size. pt.alpha Point transparency. label.size Label text size. edge_top_n Maximum number network edges draw. edge_threshold Minimum absolute edge weight plot_type = "network". NULL, largest edge_top_n edges used. arrow.length Arrow head length plot_type = "manifold". arrow.linewidth Arrow line width plot_type = "manifold". arrow.alpha Arrow transparency plot_type = "manifold". start.pt.size WT-position point size plot_type = "manifold". end.pt.size KO-position point size plot_type = "manifold". manifold.aspect.ratio Panel aspect ratio plot_type = "manifold". Default 1 keeps panel square. Set NULL use device aspect ratio. cols.sig Color significant genes. cols.ns Color non-significant genes. cols.ko Color knocked-genes. xlab, ylab Axis labels. title Plot title. NULL, default title used. theme_use Theme function used style plot. theme_args arguments passed theme_use. verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE.

title: scTenifoldKnk Plot — scTenifoldKnkPlot
text: ggplot object.

title: scTenifoldKnk Plot — scTenifoldKnkPlot
code: data(pancreas_sub) gene_use <- "Pdx1" counts <- GetAssayData5(pancreas_sub, assay = "RNA", layer = "counts") detected <- names(sort(Matrix::rowSums(counts > 0), decreasing = TRUE)) features_use <- unique(c(gene_use, head(detected, 300)))  pancreas_sub <- RunscTenifoldKnk(   pancreas_sub,   gKO = gene_use,   features = features_use,   qc = FALSE,   nc_nNet = 3,   nc_nCells = 200,   td_maxIter = 200,   store_networks = TRUE,   store_manifold = TRUE )  scTenifoldKnkPlot(pancreas_sub, plot_type = "qq")  scTenifoldKnkPlot(pancreas_sub, plot_type = "effect")  scTenifoldKnkPlot(pancreas_sub, plot_type = "network")  scTenifoldKnkPlot(pancreas_sub, plot_type = "manifold")

NULL, largest edge_top_n edges used. arrow.length Arrow head length plot_type = "manifold". arrow.linewidth Arrow line width plot_type = "manifold". arrow.alpha Arrow transparency plot_type = "manifold". start.pt.size WT-position point size plot_type = "manifold". end.pt.size KO-position point size plot_type = "manifold". manifold.aspect.ratio Panel aspect ratio plot_type = "manifold". Default 1 keeps panel square. Set NULL use device aspect ratio. cols.sig Color significant genes. cols.ns Color non-significant genes. xlab, ylab Axis labels. title Plot title. title: Seurat v5 scVI integration — scVI5_integrate
code: scVI5_integrate(   srt_merge = NULL,   batch = NULL,   append = TRUE,   srt_list = NULL,   assay = NULL,   do_normalization = NULL,   normalization_method = "LogNormalize",   do_HVF_finding = TRUE,   HVF_source = "separate",   HVF_method = "vst",   nHVF = 2000,   HVF_min_intersection = 1,   HVF = NULL,   scVI_dims_use = NULL,   nonlinear_reduction = "umap",   nonlinear_reduction_dims = c(2, 3),   nonlinear_reduction_params = list(),   force_nonlinear_reduction = TRUE,   neighbor_metric = "euclidean",   neighbor_k = 20L,   cluster_algorithm = "louvain",   cluster_resolution = 0.6,   cores = NULL,   IntegrateLayers_params = list(),   verbose = TRUE,   seed = 11 )

title: Seurat v5 scVI integration — scVI5_integrate
text: srt_merge merged `Seurat` object includes batch information. batch character string specifying batch variable name. append Whether integrated data appended original Seurat object (srt_merge). Default TRUE. srt_list list Seurat objects checked preprocessed. assay assay use. NULL, default assay Seurat object used. object also contains ChromatinAssay, default assay additional ChromatinAssay preprocessed sequentially. do_normalization Whether data normalization performed. Default TRUE. normalization_method normalization method used. Possible values "LogNormalize", "SCT", "TFIDF", "scran". Default "LogNormalize". do_HVF_finding Whether perform high variable feature finding. TRUE, function force find highly variable features (HVF) using specified HVF method. HVF_source source highly variable features. Possible values "global" "separate". Default "separate". HVF_method method use finding highly variable features. Options "vst", "mvp", "disp", "scran". Default "vst". nHVF number highly variable features select. NULL, highly variable features used. Default 2000. HVF_min_intersection feature needs present batches minimum number times order considered highly variable. Default 1. HVF vector feature names use highly variable features. NULL, function use highly variable features identified HVF method. scVI_dims_use vector specifying integrated dimensions used downstream clustering nonlinear reduction. nonlinear_reduction nonlinear dimensionality reduction method use. Options "umap", "umap-naive", "tsne", "dm", "phate", "pacmap", "trimap", "largevis", "fr". Default "umap". nonlinear_reduction_dims number dimensions keep nonlinear dimensionality reduction. vector provided, different numbers dimensions can specified method. Default c(2, 3). nonlinear_reduction_params list parameters pass nonlinear dimensionality reduction method. force_nonlinear_reduction Whether force nonlinear dimensionality reduction even specified reduction already present Seurat object. Default TRUE. neighbor_metric distance metric use finding neighbors. Options "euclidean", "cosine", "manhattan", "hamming". Default "euclidean". neighbor_k number nearest neighbors use finding neighbors. Default 20. cluster_algorithm clustering algorithm use. Options "louvain", "slm", "leiden". Default "louvain". cluster_resolution resolution parameter use clustering. Larger values result fewer clusters. Default 0.6. cores Number DataLoader worker processes scVI training. NULL (default) uses PyTorch default (0, single-process). Increase speed data loading multi-core machines. IntegrateLayers_params list parameters passed [Seurat::IntegrateLayers]. verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE. seed Random seed reproducibility. Default 11.

title: The scVI integration function — scVI_integrate
code: scVI_integrate(   srt_merge = NULL,   batch = NULL,   append = TRUE,   srt_list = NULL,   assay = NULL,   do_normalization = NULL,   normalization_method = "LogNormalize",   do_HVF_finding = TRUE,   HVF_source = "separate",   HVF_method = "vst",   nHVF = 2000,   HVF_min_intersection = 1,   HVF = NULL,   scVI_dims_use = NULL,   nonlinear_reduction = "umap",   nonlinear_reduction_dims = c(2, 3),   nonlinear_reduction_params = list(),   force_nonlinear_reduction = TRUE,   neighbor_metric = "euclidean",   neighbor_k = 20L,   cluster_algorithm = "louvain",   cluster_resolution = 0.6,   model = "SCVI",   SCVI_params = list(),   PEAKVI_params = list(),   POISSONVI_params = list(),   train_params = list(),   cores = 1,   verbose = TRUE,   seed = 11 )

title: The scVI integration function — scVI_integrate
text: srt_merge merged `Seurat` object includes batch information. batch character string specifying batch variable name. append Whether integrated data appended original Seurat object (srt_merge). Default TRUE. srt_list list Seurat objects checked preprocessed. assay assay use. NULL, default assay Seurat object used. object also contains ChromatinAssay, default assay additional ChromatinAssay preprocessed sequentially. do_normalization Whether data normalization performed. Default TRUE. normalization_method normalization method used. Possible values "LogNormalize", "SCT", "TFIDF", "scran". Default "LogNormalize". do_HVF_finding Whether perform high variable feature finding. TRUE, function force find highly variable features (HVF) using specified HVF method. HVF_source source highly variable features. Possible values "global" "separate". Default "separate". HVF_method method use finding highly variable features. Options "vst", "mvp", "disp", "scran". Default "vst". nHVF number highly variable features select. NULL, highly variable features used. Default 2000. HVF_min_intersection feature needs present batches minimum number times order considered highly variable. Default 1. HVF vector feature names use highly variable features. NULL, function use highly variable features identified HVF method. scVI_dims_use vector specifying dimensions returned scVI utilized downstream cell cluster finding nonlinear reduction. set NULL, returned dimensions used default. nonlinear_reduction nonlinear dimensionality reduction method use. Options "umap", "umap-naive", "tsne", "dm", "phate", "pacmap", "trimap", "largevis", "fr". Default "umap". nonlinear_reduction_dims number dimensions keep nonlinear dimensionality reduction. vector provided, different numbers dimensions can specified method. Default c(2, 3). nonlinear_reduction_params list parameters pass nonlinear dimensionality reduction method. force_nonlinear_reduction Whether force nonlinear dimensionality reduction even specified reduction already present Seurat object. Default TRUE. neighbor_metric distance metric use finding neighbors. Options "euclidean", "cosine", "manhattan", "hamming". Default "euclidean". neighbor_k number nearest neighbors use finding neighbors. Default 20. cluster_algorithm clustering algorithm use. Options "louvain", "slm", "leiden". Default "louvain". cluster_resolution resolution parameter use clustering. Larger values result fewer clusters. Default 0.6. model string indicating scVI model used. Options "SCVI", "PEAKVI", "POISSONVI". Default `"SCVI"`. SCVI_params list parameters SCVI model. Default `list()`. PEAKVI_params list parameters PEAKVI model. Default `list()`. POISSONVI_params list parameters POISSONVI model. Default `list()`. train_params list parameters passed model `train()` method. Default `list()`. cores integer setting number threads `scVI`. Default `1`. verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE. seed Random seed reproducibility. Default 11.

title: The scVI integration function — scVI_integrate
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data("pbmcmultiome_sub", package = "scop") pbmcmultiome_sub$batch <- rep(c("batch1", "batch2"), length.out = ncol(pbmcmultiome_sub)) pbmcmultiome_sub <- scVI_integrate(   srt_merge = pbmcmultiome_sub,   batch = "batch",   assay = "peaks",   model = "PEAKVI",   train_params = list(max_epochs = 2L) ) pbmcmultiome_sub <- scVI_integrate(   srt_merge = pbmcmultiome_sub,   batch = "batch",   assay = "peaks",   model = "POISSONVI",   train_params = list(max_epochs = 2L) ) } # }

title: Convert a Seurat object to an AnnData object — srt_to_adata

Default "RNA". layer_x Layer name assay_x Seurat object. Default "counts". assay_y Assays convert layers anndata object. Default c("spliced", "unspliced"). layer_y Layer names assay_y Seurat object. Default "counts". reductions Character vector specifying Seurat reductions convert obsm. Default NULL, converts available reductions. graphs Character vector specifying Seurat graphs convert obsp. Default NULL, converts available graphs. neighbors Character vector specifying Seurat neighbor objects convert obsp. Default NULL, converts available neighbor objects. convert_tools Whether convert tool-specific data. Default FALSE. convert_misc Whether convert miscellaneous data. Default FALSE. verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE.

title: Standard workflow for scop — standard_scop
text: function performs standard single-cell spot-level spatial analysis workflow.

title: Standard workflow for scop — standard_scop
code: standard_scop(   srt,   prefix = "Standard",   workflow = c("single_cell", "spatial"),   assay = NULL,   image = NULL,   coord.cols = c("x", "y"),   do_spot_qc = TRUE,   spot_qc_params = list(),   do_spatial_variable_features = TRUE,   spatial_variable_features_params = list(),   do_spatial_cluster = FALSE,   spatial_cluster_method = "BayesSpace",   spatial_q = NULL,   bayesspace_params = list(),   reference = NULL,   reference_label = NULL,   reference_assay = NULL,   do_deconvolution = !is.null(reference),   deconvolution_method = "RCTD",   deconvolution_params = list(),   do_normalization = NULL,   normalization_method = "LogNormalize",   do_HVF_finding = TRUE,   HVF_method = "vst",   nHVF = 2000,   HVF = NULL,   do_scaling = TRUE,   vars_to_regress = NULL,   regression_model = "linear",   linear_reduction = "pca",   linear_reduction_dims = 50,   linear_reduction_dims_use = NULL,   linear_reduction_params = list(),   force_linear_reduction = FALSE,   nonlinear_reduction = "umap",   nonlinear_reduction_dims = c(2, 3),   nonlinear_reduction_params = list(),   force_nonlinear_reduction = TRUE,   neighbor_metric = "euclidean",   neighbor_k = 20L,   cluster_algorithm = "louvain",   cluster_resolution = 0.6,   cores = 1L,   verbose = TRUE,   seed = 11,   ... )

title: Standard workflow for scop — standard_scop
text: srt Seurat object. prefix prefix add names intermediate objects created function. Default "Standard". workflow Workflow run. "single_cell" keeps original standard workflow. "spatial" runs spot-level spatial workflow wraps original workflow spot QC, spatial variable features, optional spatial clustering, optional RCTD deconvolution. assay assay use. NULL, default assay Seurat object used. object also contains ChromatinAssay, default assay additional ChromatinAssay preprocessed sequentially. image Name Seurat spatial image used spatial workflow. NULL, first image used present. coord.cols Metadata coordinate columns used spatial workflow image available. do_spot_qc Whether run RunSpotQC() spatial workflow. spot_qc_params Named list additional arguments passed RunSpotQC(). do_spatial_variable_features Whether run RunSpatialVariableFeatures() spatial workflow. spatial_variable_features_params Named list additional arguments passed RunSpatialVariableFeatures(). do_spatial_cluster Whether run spatial-aware clustering spatial workflow. spatial_cluster_method Spatial clustering method. "BayesSpace" supported workflow. spatial_q Number spatial clusters RunBayesSpace(). NULL, number ordinary spot clusters used. bayesspace_params Named list additional arguments passed RunBayesSpace(). reference Optional single-cell reference used spatial deconvolution. reference_label Metadata column reference containing cell type labels. reference_assay Assay used reference deconvolution. do_deconvolution Whether run deconvolution spatial workflow. NULL, deconvolution run reference provided. deconvolution_method Deconvolution method. One "RCTD" "SPOTlight". deconvolution_params Named list additional arguments passed RunRCTD() RunSPOTlight(). do_normalization Whether perform normalization. NULL, normalization performed specified assay scaled data. normalization_method method use normalization. Options "LogNormalize", "SCT", "TFIDF", "scran". "SCT" used RNA assay, downstream reductions clustering run generated "SCT" assay. Default "LogNormalize". do_HVF_finding Whether perform high variable feature finding. TRUE, function force find highly variable features (HVF) using specified HVF method. HVF_method method use finding highly variable features. Options "vst", "mvp", "disp", "scran". Default "vst". nHVF number highly variable features select. NULL, highly variable features used. Default 2000. HVF vector feature names use highly variable features. NULL, function use highly variable features identified HVF method. do_scaling Whether perform scaling. TRUE, function force scaling package ScaleData path. vars_to_regress vector feature names use regressors scaling step. NULL, regressors used. regression_model regression model use scaling. Options "linear", "poisson", "negativebinomial". Default "linear". linear_reduction linear dimensionality reduction method use. Options "pca", "svd", "ica", "nmf", "mds", "glmpca". Default "pca". linear_reduction_dims number dimensions keep linear dimensionality reduction. Default 50. linear_reduction_dims_use dimensions use downstream analysis. NULL, estimated dimensions stored linear reduction used available; otherwise, first 50 dimensions used fallback. linear_reduction_params list parameters pass linear dimensionality reduction method. force_linear_reduction Whether force linear dimensionality reduction even specified reduction already present Seurat object. nonlinear_reduction nonlinear dimensionality reduction method use. Options "umap", "umap-naive", "tsne", "dm", "phate", "pacmap", "trimap", "largevis", "fr". Default "umap". nonlinear_reduction_dims number dimensions keep nonlinear dimensionality reduction. vector provided, different numbers dimensions can specified method. Default c(2, 3). nonlinear_reduction_params list parameters pass nonlinear dimensionality reduction method. force_nonlinear_reduction Whether force nonlinear dimensionality reduction even specified reduction already present Seurat object. Default TRUE. neighbor_metric distance metric use finding neighbors. Options "euclidean", "cosine", "manhattan", "hamming". Default "euclidean". neighbor_k number nearest neighbors use finding neighbors. Default 20. cluster_algorithm clustering algorithm use. Options "louvain", "slm", "leiden". Default "louvain". cluster_resolution resolution parameter use clustering. Larger values result fewer clusters. Default 0.6. cores Number CPU cores used supported preprocessing steps. Default 1. verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE. seed Random seed reproducibility. Default 11. ... Additional parameters pass dimensionality reduction methods.

title: Standard workflow for scop — standard_scop
text: Seurat object.

title: Standard workflow for scop — standard_scop
code: library(Matrix) data(pancreas_sub) pancreas_sub <- standard_scop(pancreas_sub) CellDimPlot(   pancreas_sub,   group.by = "SubCellType" )  # Use a combination of different linear # or nonlinear dimension reduction methods linear_reductions <- c(   "pca", "nmf", "mds" ) pancreas_sub <- standard_scop(   pancreas_sub,   linear_reduction = linear_reductions,   nonlinear_reduction = "umap" ) plist1 <- lapply(   linear_reductions, function(lr) {     CellDimPlot(       pancreas_sub,       group.by = "SubCellType",       reduction = paste0(         "Standard", lr, "UMAP2D"       ),       xlab = "", ylab = "",       title = paste0(lr, "_umap"),       legend.position = "none",       theme_use = "theme_blank"     )   } ) patchwork::wrap_plots(plist1)  nonlinear_reductions <- c(   "umap", "tsne", "fr" ) pancreas_sub <- standard_scop(   pancreas_sub,   linear_reduction = "pca",   nonlinear_reduction = nonlinear_reductions ) plist2 <- lapply(   nonlinear_reductions, function(nr) {     CellDimPlot(       pancreas_sub,       group.by = "SubCellType",       reduction = paste0(         "Standardpca", nr, "2D"       ),       xlab = "", ylab = "",       title = paste0("pca_", nr),       legend.position = "none",       theme_use = "theme_blank"     )   } ) patchwork::wrap_plots(plist2)  if (requireNamespace("scran", quietly = TRUE)) {   data(pancreas_sub)   pancreas_scran <- pancreas_sub[, 1:80]   pancreas_scran <- standard_scop(     pancreas_scran,     assay = "RNA",     do_normalization = TRUE,     normalization_method = "scran",     do_HVF_finding = TRUE,     HVF_method = "scran",     nHVF = 100,     linear_reduction_dims = 10,     linear_reduction_dims_use = 1:5,     nonlinear_reduction = "umap",     nonlinear_reduction_dims = 2   )   CellDimPlot(     pancreas_scran,     reduction = "StandardUMAP2D",     group.by = "Standardclusters"   ) }  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(visium_human_pancreas_sub) spatial <- standard_scop(   visium_human_pancreas_sub,   workflow = "spatial",   assay = "Spatial",   do_spatial_cluster = FALSE,   spatial_cluster_method = "BayesSpace",   do_deconvolution = FALSE,   deconvolution_method = "RCTD",   linear_reduction_dims = 10,   linear_reduction_dims_use = 1:5,   nonlinear_reduction_dims = 2,   spatial_variable_features_params = list(nfeatures = 50) ) SpatialSpotPlot(spatial, group.by = "SpotQC") SpatialSpotPlot(spatial, group.by = "Standardclusters") SpatialSpotPlot(   spatial,   features = spatial@misc[["SpatialVariableFeatures"]][1:2] )  spatial_bayes <- standard_scop(   visium_human_pancreas_sub,   workflow = "spatial",   assay = "Spatial",   do_spatial_cluster = TRUE,   spatial_cluster_method = "BayesSpace",   spatial_q = 3,   do_deconvolution = FALSE,   deconvolution_method = "RCTD",   bayesspace_params = list(     n.PCs = 5,     n.HVGs = 200,     store_sce = FALSE,     spatial_cluster_params = list(       nrep = 200,       burn.in = 50,       thin = 10,       save.chain = FALSE     )   ) ) SpatialSpotPlot(spatial_bayes, group.by = "BayesSpace_cluster")  data(panc8_sub) spatial_rctd <- standard_scop(   visium_human_pancreas_sub,   workflow = "spatial",   assay = "Spatial",   do_spatial_cluster = FALSE,   spatial_cluster_method = "BayesSpace",   do_deconvolution = TRUE,   deconvolution_method = "RCTD",   reference = panc8_sub,   reference_assay = "RNA",   reference_label = "celltype",   deconvolution_params = list(     max_cores = 1,     min_cells = 25   ) ) SpatialSpotPlot(spatial_rctd, group.by = "RCTD_dominant_type")  rctd_cols <- grep(   "^RCTD_prop_",   colnames(spatial_rctd@meta.data),   value = TRUE ) SpatialSpotPlot(   spatial_rctd,   group.by = rctd_cols[1:min(4, length(rctd_cols))] ) SpatialSpotPlot(   spatial_rctd,   group.by = "RCTD_dominant_type",   plot_type = "pie" ) } # }

title: Plot tAge transcriptomic aging-clock predictions — tAgePlot
text: object Seurat object returned RuntAge() data frame tAge prediction columns. score_col Prediction column plot. NULL, first column ending "_tAge" used. group.Column used group predictions y axis. split.optional column split plot facets. tool_name Name Seurat tool entry containing tAge results. plot_type Plot type. Currently "box" draws boxplot jittered pseudobulk samples. palette, palcolor Palette forwarded thisplot::palette_colors(). alpha Overall point box alpha. point_alpha box_alpha take precedence set. point_size, point_alpha Jittered point size alpha. box_alpha Boxplot fill alpha. flip Whether flip coordinates group labels run along y axis. Default TRUE. title, subtitle, xlab, ylab Plot labels. theme_use Theme function name. Default "theme_scop", maps thisplot::theme_this(), matching style used thisplot::StatPlot(). theme_args Additional arguments passed theme_use. legend.position, legend.direction Legend position direction. Default "none". grid_major Whether show major panel grid lines. grid_major_colour, grid_major_linetype, grid_major_linewidth Appearance major panel grid lines. aspect.ratio Aspect ratio plot (y / x). seed RNG seed jitter reproducibility. verbose Whether print message. Default TRUE. ... Reserved future use.

text: compact gene-filtered version human pancreatic intraepithelial neoplasia (PanIN) 10x Visium dataset GSE254829. object keeps 1986 non-background tissue spots sample GSM8058244 (PanIN-LG2), Spatial assay, slice1 Visium image, tissue coordinates metadata columns x y. Metadata column coda_label stores dominant CODA microanatomical component spot, coda_score stores percentage. Component percentage columns stored coda_ prefix, matched CODA table stored @tools$GSE254829_coda_table. keep package data small directly usable bundled panc8_sub reference, object retains top 5000 genes shared panc8_sub, ranked total spatial counts.
